  DIVR_NFC[1:L] <<- (ea$DIVR_NFC[1:L]*(1-theta_psb))/1000#Dividends received by the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  DIVP_NFC[1:L] <<- (ea$DIVP_NFC[1:L]*(1-theta_psb))/1000#Dividends paid by the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  GCF_NFC[1:L] <<- {x <- (ea$GCF_NFC[1:L]-GCF_PS[1:L])/1000; gr <- mean(diff(x[(L-4):L])/x[(L-4):(L-1)]); x[(L-3):L] <- x[L-4] * (1+gr)^(1:4); x}#Gross capital formation of the NFC sector (£ billion)   ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  GCF_NFCG[1:L] <<- (10.5/4) #Green gross capital formation of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Based on UK Government Green Financing: Allocation Report 2024
  LEND_NFC[1:L] <<- ea$LEND_NFC[1:L]/1000 - LEND_PS[1:L]#Net-lending position of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  eta_NFCT<<- mean(DISC_NFC[41:144]/GDP[41:144])#Long run lending discrepancy of NFC sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  eta_RoWT<<- mean(DISC_RoW[41:144]/GDP[41:144])#Long run lending discrepancy of RoW sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  IBATR_NFC[1:L] <<- ((ea$F2ATR_NFC[1:L]+ea$F3ATR_NFC[1:L]+ea$F4ATR_NFC[1:L])*(1-theta_psb))/1000 #Interest bearing asset net-transfers of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBLTR_NFC[1:L] <<- ((ea$F2LTR_NFC[1:L]+ea$F3LTR_NFC[1:L]+ea$F4LTR_NFC[1:L])*(1-theta_psb))/1000#Interest bearing liabilities net-transfers of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  EQATR_NFC[1:L] <<- ea$F5ATR_NFC[1:L]*((1-theta_psb)/1000)  #Equity asset net-transfers of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  EQLTR_NFC[1:L] <<- ea$F5LTR_NFC[1:L]*((1-theta_psb)/1000) #Equity liabilities net-transfers of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBA_NFC[1:L] <<- ((ea$F2A_NFC[1:L] + ea$F3A_NFC[1:L] + ea$F4A_NFC[1:L])*(1-theta_psb))/1000 #Interest bearing assets of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBL_NFC[1:L] <<- ((ea$F2L_NFC[1:L] + ea$F3L_NFC[1:L] + ea$F4L_NFC[1:L])*(1-theta_psb))/1000 #Interest bearing liabilities of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  EQA_NFC[1:L] <<- ((ea$F5A_NFC[1:L])*(1-theta_psb))/1000 #Equity assets of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 
  EQL_NFC[1:L] <<- ((ea$F5L_NFC[1:L])*(1-theta_psb))/1000 #Equity liabilities of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  FNW_NFC[1:L] <<- ea$FNW_NFC[1:L]*((1-theta_psb)/1000)#Financial net-worth of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  delta_KP[1:L] <<- c(NaN,((mydata$CC_PRODR[2:L]-mydata$CC_K_PS[2:L]))/(mydata$K_NFC_R[1:(L-1)]+mydata$K_GVT_R[1:(L-1)]-mydata$K_PS_R[1:(L-1)])) #Depreciation rate of Production sector capital ## Calculated from the ONS capital stock tables 2023
  K_NFCR[1:L] <<- mydata$K_NFC_R[1:L]/1000#Capital stock of the NFC sector 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS capital stock tables 2023
  K_NFCGR[141:L] <<- 0.04*K_NFCR[141:L] #Green capital stock of the NFC sector 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Taken as a reasonable estimate of 4% of 2022 capital stock consistent with approximate UK green investment share from ONS data and DESNZ estimates
  LEND_MFI[1:L] <<- ea$LEND_MFI[1:L]/1000 #Net-lending position of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  eta_MFIT<<- mean(DISC_MFI[41:144]/GDP[41:144])#Long run lending discrepancy of MFI sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  eta_NFCB<<- mean(RESTR_NFC[41:144]/GDP[41:144])#Long run residual transaction discrepancy of NFC sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  eta_PSB<<- mean(RESTR_PS[41:144]/GO_PS[41:144])#Long run residual transaction discrepancy of PS sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  eta_NMFIB<<- mean(RESTR_NMFI[54:144]/GDP[54:144])#Long run residual transaction discrepancy of NMFI sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  eta_RoWB<<- mean(RESTR_RoW[54:144]/GDP[54:144])#Long run residual transaction discrepancy of RoW sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  INTR_NMFI[1:L] <<- (bbfq$INTR_NMFI1[1:L] + bbfq$INTR_NMFI2[1:L])/1000 #Interest received by the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK blue book 2023, annual data converted to quarterly using cubic spline interpolation
  INTP_NMFI[1:L] <<- (bbfq$INTP_NMFI1[1:L] + bbfq$INTP_NMFI2[1:L])/1000 #Interest paid by the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK blue book 2023, annual data converted to quarterly using cubic spline interpolation
  DIVR_HH[1:L] <<- ea$DIVR_HH[1:L]/1000#Dividends received by the Household sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBA_NMFI[1:L] <<- (ea$F2A_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F2A_NMFI2[1:L] + ea$F3A_NMFI1[1:L]+          ea$F3A_NMFI2[1:L] +ea$F4A_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F4A_NMFI2[1:L])/1000 - IBL_GVTNMFI[1:L] #Interest bearing assets of the NMFI sector (£ billion) excluding government borrowing##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBL_NMFI[1:L] <<- (ea$F2L_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F3L_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F3L_NMFI2[1:L] +ea$F4L_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F4L_NMFI2[1:L])/1000 #Interest bearing liabilities of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  PENS[1:L] <<- mydata$PENS[1:L]/1000 #Total pension scheme stock - asset of households and a liability of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK blue book accounts 2023 - converted to quarterly data using cubic spline interpolation
  INS[1:L] <<- (ea$F6A_HH[1:L] - bbsq$PENS[1:L])/1000 #Total Insurance stock - asset of households and a liability of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBL_GVT[1:L] <<- mydata$BRW_GVT_MAAS[1:L]/1000 #Interest bearing liabilities of the government sector (£ billion)##Taken from UK government Maastricht debt data
  IBL_GVTMFI[1:L] <<- beta_IBLGVTMFI*IBL_GVT[1:L]#Interest bearing liabilities of the government sector held by MFIs (£ billion)##Taken as a proportion of total government IBLs based on flow of funds data
  IBL_GVTNMFI[1:L] <<- beta_IBLGVTNMFI*IBL_GVT[1:L]#Interest bearing liabilities of the government sector held by NMFIs (£ billion)##Taken as a proportion of total government IBLs based on flow of funds data
  IBL_GVTRoW[1:L] <<- beta_IBLGVTRoW*IBL_GVT[1:L]#Interest bearing liabilities of the government sector held by RoW (£ billion)##Taken as a proportion of total government IBLs based on flow of funds data
  EQN_RoW[1:L] <<- (ea$F5A_RoW[1:L]-ea$F5L_RoW[1:L])/1000 #Equity assets of the RoW sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 
  IBATR_NMFI[1:L] <<- (ea$F2ATR_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F2ATR_NMFI2[1:L] + ea$F3ATR_NMFI1[1:L] +     ea$F3ATR_NMFI2[1:L] +ea$F4ATR_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F4ATR_NMFI2[1:L])/1000 - IBLTR_GVTNMFI[1:L]#Interest bearing asset net-transfers of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBLTR_NMFI[1:L] <<- (ea$F2LTR_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F3LTR_NMFI1[1:L] +                      ea$F3LTR_NMFI2[1:L] +ea$F4LTR_NMFI1[1:L] + ea$F4LTR_NMFI2[1:L])/1000#Interest bearing liability net-transfers of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  INTAX_HH[1:L] <<- ea$INCTAX_HH[1:L]/1000#Income tax paid by the household sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  SOCC_NMFI[1:L] <<- ea$SOCC_NMFI[1:L]/1000#Social contributions received by the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  SOCC_GVT[1:L] <<- ea$SOCC_GVT[1:L]/1000#Social contributions received by the Government sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  SOCB_NMFI[1:L] <<- ea$SOCB_NMFI[1:L]/1000#Social benefits paid by the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  SOCB_GVT[1:L] <<- ea$SOCB_GVT[1:L]/1000#Social benefits paid by the Government sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  OCONS_GVT[1:L] <<- {x <- (ea$CONS_GVT[1:L] - bbfq$WAGES_GVT[1:L] - ea$GOS_GVT[1:L])/1000; gr <- mean(diff(x[(L-4):L])/x[(L-4):(L-1)]); x[(L-3):L] <- x[L-4] * (1+gr)^(1:4); x}#Other consumption of the Government sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 by deducting government wages and gross operating surplus from total government consumption
  GCF_GVTG[1:L] <<- (GCF_NFCG[L]/GCF_NFC[L])*GCF_GVT[L]#Gross capital formation of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Taken by assuming government has the same initial proportion of green investment as the NFC sector
  LEND_GVT[1:L] <<- ea$LEND_GVT[1:L]/1000#Net-lending position of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  eta_GVTT<<- mean(DISC_GVT[41:144]/GDP[41:144])#Long run lending discrepancy of government sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  IBA_GVT[1:L] <<- (ea$F2A_GVT[1:L] + ea$F3A_GVT[1:L] + ea$F4A_GVT[1:L])/1000 #Interest bearing assets of the government sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBATR_GVT[1:L] <<- (ea$F2ATR_GVT[1:L]+ea$F3ATR_GVT[1:L]+ea$F4ATR_GVT[1:L])/1000 #Interest bearing asset net-transfers of the GVT sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBLTR_GVT[1:L] <<- (ea$F2LTR_GVT[1:L]+ea$F3LTR_GVT[1:L]+ea$F4LTR_GVT[1:L])/1000#Interest bearing liabilities net-transfers of the GVT sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  MAAS_ADJ[1:L] <<- (ea$F2L_GVT[1:L]+ ea$F3L_GVT[1:L] + ea$F4L_GVT[1:L])/1000 - IBL_GVT[1:L] #Maastricht debt adjustment to interest bearing liability stock of the government sector ##Calculated from past data
  FNW_GVT[1:L] <<- ea$FNW_GVT[1:L]/1000 + MAAS_ADJ[1:L]#Financial net-worth of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 adjusted to account for Maastricht debt
  eta_GVTB<<- mean(RESTR_GVT[54:144]/GDP[54:144])#Long run residual transaction discrepancy of GVT sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  K_GVTR[1:L] <<- mydata$K_GVT_R[1:L]/1000#Capital stock of the GVT sector 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS capital stock tables 2023
  K_GVTGR[141:L] <<- 0.04*K_GVTR[141:L] #Green capital stock of the GVT sector 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Taken as a reasonable estimate of 4% of 2022 capital stock
  INTR_HH[1:L] <<- (ea$INTR_HH[1:L] - ea$FISIM_DEP_HH[1:L])/1000 #Interest received by the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 
  INTP_HH[1:L] <<- (ea$INTP_HH[1:L] + ea$FISIM_LOANS_HH[1:L])/1000 #Interest paid by the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  PENSR[1:L] <<- ea$PENSR[1:L]/1000#Income payable on pension entitlements##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  INSR[1:L] <<- ea$D441HH[1:L]/1000#Income payable on insurance entitlements##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  LEND_NMFI[1:L] <<- (ea$LEND_NMFI1[1:L]+ea$LEND_NMFI2[1:L])/1000 #Net-lending position of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  eta_NMFIT<<- mean(DISC_NMFI[41:L]/GDP[41:L])#Long run lending discrepancy of NMFI sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  FNW_NMFI[1:L] <<- (ea$FNW_NMFI1[1:L]+ea$FNW_NMFI2)/1000 - beta_IBLGVTNMFI*MAAS_ADJ[1:L]#Financial net-worth of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  CONS_HH[1:L] <<- {x <- (ea$CONS_HH[1:L] - ea$FISIM_DEP_HH[1:L] + ea$FISIM_LOANS_HH[1:L] + bbfq$FISIM_INTCONS_HH[1:L] - ea$GOS_HH[1:L])/1000; gr <- mean(diff(x[(L-4):L])/x[(L-4):(L-1)]); x[(L-3):L] <- x[L-4] * (1+gr)^(1:4); x} #Household consumption (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 adjusting for rents and FISIM
  CONS_HHPS[1:L] <<- P_ELEC[1:L]*E_ELECH[1:L] #Household consumption from the power sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS supply and use tables 2024
  GCF_HHNBR[1:L] <<- ea$GCF_HHNBR[1:L]/1000 #Gross fixed capital formation in new houses 2022 prices (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  beta_GCFHH[1:L] <<- 0.14 #Estimate of initial proportion of home improvement spending on energy efficiency##Estimate based on ONS data and to generate the baseline scenario
  H[1:L] <<- mydata$NDWELL[1:L]/1000000#Total number of UK properties (millions)##From UK census data
  H_EN[1:L] <<- H[1:L]*(mydata$EPC_ABC[1:L])*(1-mydata$EPC_ABC_R[1:L])#Total number of UK energy efficient non-electric properties (EPC rating C and above with non-electric primary fuel) (millions)##From UK EPC data
  H_EE[1:L] <<- H[1:L]*(mydata$EPC_ABC[1:L])*(mydata$EPC_ABC_R[1:L])#Total number of UK energy efficient electric properties (EPC rating C and above with electric primary fuel) (millions)##From UK EPC data
  H_I[1:L] <<- H[1:L]*(1-mydata$EPC_ABC[1:L])#Total number of UK non-energy efficient non-electric properties (EPC rating D and below) (millions)##From UK EPC data
  LEND_HH[1:L] <<- ea$LEND_HH[1:L]/1000 #Net-lending position of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  eta_HHT<<- mean(DISC_HH[41:144]/GDP[41:144])#Long run lending discrepancy of H sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  IBA_HH[1:L] <<- (ea$F2A_HH[1:L] + ea$F3A_HH[1:L] + ea$F4A_HH[1:L])/1000 #Interest bearing assets of the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBL_HH[1:L] <<- (ea$F3L_HH[1:L] + ea$F4L_HH[1:L])/1000 #Interest bearing liabilities of the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  EQA_HH[1:L] <<- (ea$F5A_HH[1:L])/1000 #Equity assets of the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  GCF_HHHIENR[1:L] <<- 0.095*GCF_HHHIR[1:L]#Gross fixed capital formation in energy efficiency home improvements 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated such that initial value is reasonable
  IBATR_HH[1:L] <<- (ea$F2ATR_HH[1:L]+ea$F3ATR_HH[1:L]+ea$F4ATR_HH[1:L])/1000 #Interest bearing asset net-transfers of the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBLTR_HH[1:L] <<- (ea$F3LTR_HH[1:L]+ea$F4LTR_HH[1:L])/1000#Interest bearing liabilities net-transfers of the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  EQATR_HH[1:L] <<- ea$F5ATR_HH[1:L]/1000  #Equity asset net-transfers of the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  FNW_HH[1:L] <<- ea$FNW_HH[1:L]/1000#Financial net-worth of the HH sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  PENSTR[1:L] <<- PENS_ADJ[1:L] #Total pension scheme stock net transfers - asset of households and a liability of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK blue book accounts 2023 - converted to quarterly data using cubic spline interpolation
  INSTR[1:L] <<- (ea$F6ATR_HH[1:L] - bbfq$PENSTR[1:L])/1000 #Total Insurance stock net transfers - asset of households and a liability of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  eta_HHB<<- mean(RESTR_HH[41:144]/GDP[41:144])#Long run lending discrepancy of H sector relative to GDP ##Taken as the mean of past data
  beta_HBE <<- 0.0842183#Proportion of new build properties which are fully electric##Based on implied UK data and assumed constant
  C_HIENR[1:L] <<- 7.529 #Average cost per property of upgrading from EPC D/below to EPC C/above (non-electric) in 2022 prices (£ billion per million homes, i.e. £7,529 per property)## Based on cost of energy efficiency installations at 2022 prices
  C_HIEER[1:L] <<- 10 #Average cost per property of converting an energy efficient home to full electric heating in 2022 prices (£ billion per million homes, i.e. £10,000 per property)## Based on heat pump installation cost estimates at 2022 prices
  P_H[1:L] <<- mydata$PDWELL[1:L]*1000 #House prices (£ billion per million houses)##Taken as the average house price from the UK house price index
  P_I[1:L] <<- {x <- (ea$P_IMP[1:L]/ea$P_IMP[L])*P_P[L]; gr <- mean(diff(x[(L-4):L])/x[(L-4):(L-1)]); x[(L-3):L] <- x[L-4] * (1+gr)^(1:4); x} #Import prices ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 and normalised around the initial condition
  P_F[1:L] <<- (mydata$P_RoW[1:L]/mydata$P_RoW[L]) #Global Price index ##Taken from world bank data
  P_E[1:L] <<- (ea$P_EXP[1:L]/ea$P_EXP[L])*P_P[L] #Export prices ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 and normalised around the initial condition
  INTR_GVT[1:L] <<- ea$INTR_GVT[1:L]/1000 #Interest received by the GVT sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 
  INTP_GVT[1:L] <<- ea$INTP_GVT[1:L]/1000 #Interest paid by the GVT sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  INTN_RoW[1:L] <<- (ea$INTR_RoW[1:L]-ea$INTP_RoW[1:L])/1000  #Net-Interest received by the RoW sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 
  DIVN_RoW[1:L] <<- (ea$DIVR_RoW[1:L]-ea$DIVP_RoW[1:L])/1000 #Net-Dividends received by the RoW sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024 
  LEND_RoW[1:L] <<- ea$LEND_RoW[1:L]/1000#Net-lending position of the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBNTR_RoW[1:L] <<- ((ea$F2ATR_RoW[1:L]+ea$F3ATR_RoW[1:L]+ea$F4ATR_RoW[1:L])-(ea$F2LTR_RoW[1:L]+ea$F3LTR_RoW[1:L]+ea$F4LTR_RoW[1:L]))/1000 - IBLTR_GVTRoW[1:L] #Interest bearing asset net-transfers of the RoW sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  EQNTR_RoW[1:L] <<- (ea$F5ATR_RoW[1:L]-ea$F5LTR_RoW[1:L])/1000  #Equity asset net-transfers of the RoW sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  IBLTR_GVTMFI[1:L] <<- beta_IBLGVTMFI*IBLTR_GVT[1:L]#Interest bearing liabilities transfers of the government sector held by MFIs (£ billion)##Taken as a proportion of total government IBLs based on flow of funds data
  IBLTR_GVTNMFI[1:L] <<- beta_IBLGVTNMFI*IBLTR_GVT[1:L]#Interest bearing liabilities transfers of the government sector held by NMFIs (£ billion)##Taken as a proportion of total government IBLs based on flow of funds data
  IBLTR_GVTRoW[1:L] <<- beta_IBLGVTRoW*IBLTR_GVT[1:L]#Interest bearing liabilities transfers of the government sector held by RoW (£ billion)##Taken as a proportion of total government IBLs based on flow of funds data
  IBN_RoW[1:L] <<- ((ea$F2A_RoW[1:L] + ea$F3A_RoW[1:L] + ea$F4A_RoW[1:L])-(ea$F2L_RoW[1:L] + ea$F3L_RoW[1:L] + ea$F4L_RoW[1:L]))/1000 - IBL_GVTRoW[1:L] #Interest bearing assets of the RoW sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  FNW_RoW[1:L] <<- ea$FNW_RoW[1:L]/1000 - beta_IBLGVTRoW*MAAS_ADJ[1:L] #Financial net-worth of the RoW sector (£ billion)##Taken from the ONS UK economic accounts 2024
  alpha_1CHHP <<- (1-0.186429)#Autoregressive parameter ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_2CHHP <<- 0.109223 #Long run propensity to consume out of disposable income ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_3CHHP <<- 0.054266 #Long run propensity to consume out of household financial assets##Econometrically estimated
  CR_NFC[1:L] <<- c(rep(0.1,81),(1-mydata$CR_NFC[82:L])) #Credit rationing rate for NFCs ## Constructed from credit conditions index while assuming maximum rationing rate of 40%
  CR_PS[1:L] <<- c(rep(0.1,81),(1-mydata$CR_NFC[82:L])) #Credit rationing rate for power sector ## Constructed from credit conditions index while assuming maximum rationing rate of 40%
  r_BOE[1:L] <<- mydata$R_BOE[1:L] #Bank of England annual base rate ##From Bank of England data
  INF_A[1:L] <<- mydata$INF[1:L]/100 #UK annual CPI inflation rate ##From ONS CPI data
  P_ETS[1:L] <<- 0.006412537*P[1:L] #ETS Price £ bn/MtCO2e adjusted for 2024 pricing ##Based on initial government income from the ETS scheme in 2022
  COV_ETSP[1:L] <<- 0.1882468 #Proportion of production emissions subject to ETS ##Taken as a rate consistent with UK ETS revenue in 2022
  COV_ETSPS[1:L] <<- 0.5 #Proportion of power sector emissions subject to ETS ##Taken as an estimate based on OBR emission and tax forecasts
  P_NELEC[1:L] <<- ((mydata$COST_NELEC/1000)/E_NELEC[1:L]) #Price of  non-electricity energy (£billion/TwH)##Calculated by dividing total non-electric costs from DUKES table 1.1.6 by non-electric energy use
  P_GAS[1:L] <<- mydata$P_GAS[1:L]*P_P[1:L] #Wholesale gas price in the UK ##From OBR estimates 2024
  P_OIL[1:L] <<- mydata$P_OIL[1:L]*P_P[1:L] #Wholesale oil price in the UK ##From OBR estimates 2024
  CF_FF <<- 1.131779 #Capacity factor of fossil fuel energy generation## Calculated based on past values and assumed to be constant over time
  GVT_ENERGY<<- 0 # ##
  GREEN_POWER<<- 0 # ##
  ALT_PRICING <<- 0 # ##
  GBE_END <<- (L+29) # ##
  HOUSING_SUB <<- 0 # ##
  FF_BAN <<- 0 # ##
  GVT_CRED <<- 0 # ##
  POWER_SUB <<- 0 # ##
  TRANS_PASS <<- 0 # ##
  CRED <<- 1 # ##
  FF_INV_BAN <<- 0 # ##
  TTRANS <<- 1 # ##
  TTRANSH <<- 0 # ##
  FF_CAP_BAN <<- L+T+10 # ##
  HOUSING_SUB_RATE <<- 0 # ##
  GVT_INVEST <<- 0 # ##
  GDPPLOT_R[1:L] <<- GDP_R[1:L]*4 # ##
  BETA_ELECPLOT[1:L] <<- (E_ELECNFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L])*100 # ##
  DEBT_GDP[1:L] <<- (-FNW_GVT[1:L]/(GDP[1:L]*4))*100 #Debt to GDP ratio (%) ##Calculated from initial Government debt and GDP levels
  W_SPLOT[1:L] <<- W_S[1:L]*100 # ##
  DEF_GDP[1:L] <<- (-LEND_GVTM[1:L]/(epsilon_GDP*GDP[1:L]))*100 # ##
  P_ETSPLOT[1:L] <<- P_ETS[1:L]*1000 # ##
  EMISPLOT[1:L] <<- EMIS[1:L]*4 # ##
  GCF_PSNFFRPLOT[1:L] <<- GCF_PSNFFR[1:L]*4 # ##
  GCF_NFFTOTPLOT[1:L] <<- (GCF_PSNFFR[1:L] + SPEND_GVTPS[1:L]/P_P[1:L])*4 # ##
  GCF_HHHIERPLOT[1:L] <<- GCF_HHHIER[1:L]*4 # ##
  UPLOT[1:L] <<- ru[1:L]*100 # ##
  GCF_GRPLOT[1:L] <<- (GCF_HHHIEER[1:L] + GCF_NFCGR[1:L] + GCF_GVTGR[1:L] + GCF_PSNFFR[1:L])*4 # ##
  alpha_1lambda <<- 0.725 #Parameter in the productivity equation relating real GDP growth to productivity growth ##Reasonable estimate based on past values, UK studies and calibrated to generate the baseline projections
  alpha_2lambda <<- 0.025 #Parameter in the productivity equation relating real GCF growth to productivity growth ##Reasonable estimate based on past values and UK studies
  spr_hha <<- mean(r_BOEQ[2:88]-r_IBAHH[2:88]) #Mark down on household deposit interest rates ## Based on average of the pre 2010s zero lower bound period
  spr_nfca <<- mean(r_BOEQ[2:88]-r_IBANFC[2:88]) #Mark down on NFC deposit interest rates ## Based on average of the pre 2010s zero lower bound period
  spr_gvta <<- mean(r_BOEQ[2:140]-r_IBAGVT[2:140]) #Mark down on government deposit interest rates ## Based on average of the pre 2010s zero lower bound period
  spr_nmfia <<- mean(r_BOEQ[54:88]-r_IBANMFI[54:88]) #Mark down on NMFI deposit interest rates ## Based on average of the pre 2010s zero lower bound period
  spr_rowa <<- mean(r_BOEQ[100:140]-r_IBNRoW[100:140]) #Mark down on RoW deposit interest rates ## Based on average of the pre 2010s zero lower bound period
  minr_hh <<- min(mean(r_IBAHH[89:140]),r_IBAHH[L]) #Minimum lower bound household deposit interest rate ## Calculated based on the 2010s zero lower bound interest rate period
  minr_nfc <<- min(mean(r_IBANFC[89:140]),r_IBANFC[L]) #Minimum lower bound NFC deposit interest rate ## Calculated based on the 2010s zero lower bound interest rate period
  minr_gvt <<- min(mean(r_IBAGVT[89:140]),r_IBAGVT[L]) #Minimum lower bound GVT deposit interest rate ## Calculated based on the 2010s zero lower bound interest rate period
  minr_nmfi <<- min(mean(r_IBANMFI[89:140]),r_IBANMFI[L]) #Minimum lower bound NMFI deposit interest rate ## Calculated based on the 2010s zero lower bound interest rate period
  minr_row <<- min(mean(r_IBNRoW[89:140]),r_IBNRoW[L]) #Minimum lower bound RoW deposit interest rate ## Calculated based on the 2010s zero lower bound interest rate period
  FNW_MFIT <<- mean(FNW_MFI[88:144]/FL_MFI[88:144]) #Target MFI FNW ratio ##Calculated based on recent data
  POP[1:L] <<- mydata$POP[1:L] #Total 16+ population (millions) ##Taken from OBR data
  spr_nfcl <<- -0.000832086  #Interest rate spread on NFC liabilities interest rates ##Based on recent values
  spr_psl <<- -0.000832086 #Interest rate spread on PS liabilities interest rates ##Based on recent values
  spr_hhl <<- 0.005770237  #Interest rate spread on HH liabilities interest rates ##Based on recent values
  spr_gvtl <<- 0.001794094 #Interest rate spread on GVT liabilities interest rates ##Based on recent values
  spr_nmfil <<- 0.00164131 #Interest rate spread on NMFI liabilities interest rates ##Based on recent values
  sigma_nfc<<- 34.85146 #Parameter driving the size of the interest rate spread on NFC liabilities ##Based on recent values
  sigma_ps<<-  34.85146  #Parameter driving the size of the interest rate spread on PS liabilities ##Based on recent values
  sigma_hh<<- 24.3022  #Parameter driving the size of the interest rate spread on HH liabilities ##Based on recent values
  sigma_gvt<<- -22.92383   #Parameter driving the size of the interest rate spread on GVT liabilities ##Based on recent values
  sigma_nmfi<<- 21.62897   #Parameter driving the size of the interest rate spread on NMFI liabilities ##Based on recent values
  COST_NELEC[1:L] <<- (mydata$COST_NELEC[1:L]/1000) #Cost of non-electric energy (£ billion) ##Taken from DUKES table 1.1.6 non-electric energy cost
  sigma_ELEC   <<- 0.975 #Upper bound of non-fossil fuel electricity share (0–1); set at 0.975 to preserve a minimal fossil fuel backstop for grid stability ##Consistent with UK National Grid assumptions
  ELEC_BUFFER  <<- 0     #Reserve margin added to E_ELECMAX (0 = none); set >0 in scenarios prone to cusp oscillation at the supply constraint boundary ##Default zero; no effect on standard scenarios
  delta_kpc <<- 0.02375083 #Constant depreciation rate of productive capital ##Calculated from implied rates based on capital stock data
  beta_NELECGAS <<- 0.55 #Proportion of non-electric energy provided by gas ##Taken from 2023 data and assumed constant
  beta_NELECOIL <<- 0.45 #Proportion of non-electric energy provided by oil ##Taken from 2023 data and assumed constant
  alpha_NELEC <<- mean(P_NELEC[85:L]/(beta_NELECGAS*P_GAS[85:L]+beta_NELECOIL*P_OIL[85:L])) #Pass through from gas and oil prices to overall non-electric energy costs ##Calculated using the mean over past data
  alpha_1WS <<- 1.75865#Unemployment rate parameter in the wage share equation ##Econometrically estimated
  mu_MCELEC <<- 0.325 #Parameter relating the marginal cost of fossil fuel electricty to the overall marginal cost of electricity ##Set as a reasonable estimate to generate the baseline scenario
  MU_ELEC <<- 1.22773  # Mark up of electricity prices above the marginal cost of electricity production ##Calculated based on past data
  alpha_DIVPPS <<- mean(DIVP_PS[51:144]/GO_PS[51:144]) #Rate of power sector dividend payments relative to output ##Set as the mean of past implied values
  alpha_DIVPNFC <<-  mean(DIVP_NFC[124:144]/YD_NFC[124:144]) # mean(DIVP_NFC[51:144]/YD_NFC[51:144]) #Rate of NFC sector dividend payments relative to disposable income ##Set as the mean of past implied values
  alpha_PFUEL <<- 10.97642 #Relationship between wholesale gas prices and the price of a fuel input to the power sector ##Based on initial data
  alpha_1GCFNFC <<-  0.029485 #Parameter in the NFC gross capital formation equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_2GCFNFC <<- 0.304866 #Parameter in the NFC gross capital formation equation ##Econometrically estimated
  #alpha_0GCFNFC calibrated after u_NFC_W is seeded (see below)
  alpha_1betaNFC <<- 0.1 #Parameter in the equation governing the proportion of gross green NFC gross capital formation  ## Calibrated to generate the baseline scenario
  alpha_2betaNFC <<- 0.4 #Parameter in the equation governing the proportion of gross green NFC gross capital formation  ## Calibrated to generate the baseline scenario
  alpha_0betaNFC <<- beta_nfc[L] - alpha_1betaNFC * tanh(P_NELECT[L] / P_ELEC[L]) - alpha_2betaNFC * (COST_ER[L-1] / E[L-1]) #Parameter in the equation governing the proportion of gross green NFC gross capital formation  ## Calibrated to generate the baseline scenario
  alpha_DIVPNMFI <<- mean(DIVP_NMFI[1:L]/((DIVR_NMFI[1:L] + INTR_NMFI[1:L]-INTP_NMFI[1:L]))) #Proportion of primary NMFI net income distributed as dividends ##Set as the mean over past values
  alpha_PENSR <<- 1.212978 #Parameter relating NMFI disposable income to investment income on pension schemes ##Calculated based on past implied values
  alpha_INSR <<- 1.371938 #Parameter relating NMFI disposable income to investment income on insurance schemes ##Calculated based on past implied values
  tau_gvt <<- 0.05 #Adjustment speed to long run government income and spending rates ##Set based on reasonable estimate and to generate the baseline scenario
  ITAXR_PLR <<- mean(ITAXR_P[141:144]) #Long run indirect tax rate on production set by the government ##Based on past data and OBR fiscal forecasts
  ITAXR_PSLR <<- mean(ITAXR_PS[101:132]) #Long run indirect tax rate on the power sector set by the government ##Based on past data and OBR fiscal forecasts
  INTAXR_NFCLR <<-  (mean(INTAXR_NFC[141:144]))  #Long run income tax rate on the NFC sector set by the government ##Based on past data and OBR fiscal forecasts
  INTAXR_HHLR <<- (mean(INTAXR_HH[141:144])) #Long run income tax rate on the HH sector set by the government ##Based on past data and OBR fiscal forecasts
  SOCCR_GVTLR <<-  (mean(SOCCR_GVT[101:132]))  #Long run social contribution rate on the HH sector set by the government ##Based on past data and OBR fiscal forecasts
  alpha_0SOCB <<- 0.060097#Parameter in the GVT social benefit equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_1SOCB <<- 1-0.468271#Parameter in the GVT social benefit equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_2SOCB <<- 0.139188#Parameter in the GVT social benefit equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_OCONSGVT <<- OCONS_GVT[L]/GDP[L-1] #Parameter relating GDP output to total government other consumption ##Set based on OBR projections of government spending and past 
  alpha_0GCFNB <<- 7.679432 #Parameter in the HH gross capital formation for new builds equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_1GCFNB <<- 1.224157 #Parameter in the HH gross capital formation for new builds equation ##Econometrically estimated
  delta_1GCFNB <<- 0.16469 #Parameter in the HH gross capital formation for new builds equation ##Econometrically estimated
  epsilon_GCFNB <<- 0.56456 #Parameter in the HH gross capital formation for new builds equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_HHHI <<- 0.008546253 #Parameter relating household home improvement investment to overall GDP levels ##Estimated based on past values
  alpha_0betaHH <<- 0.2 #Parameter in the equation for green home improvements ##Estimate based on green housing investment data
  TREND_betaHH <<- 0.00056678 #Parameter in the equation for green home improvements ##Estimate based on green housing investment data
  alpha_1betaHH <<- 0.3037772 #Parameter in the equation for green home improvements ##Estimate based on green housing investment data
  alpha_INSTR <<- 3.204082e-03 #Insurance transfer payout rate ##Calculated from past data
  alpha_IBLTR <<- 0.00175 #Parameter relating total housing value to household interest bearing liability transfers ## Calculated based on ONS stock data
  epsilon_PH <<- 0.06219 #Parameter in the house price equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_0PH <<- 1.000322 #Parameter in the house price equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_1PH <<- 1.040199 #Parameter in the house price equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_2PH <<- 0.5389934 #Parameter in the house price equation ##Econometrically estimated
  ER[1:L] <<- (mydata$ER[1:L]/mydata$ER[L]) #Nominal exchange Rate Index ##Taken from ONS exchange rate index data and normalised around initial condition
  epsilon_rboe <<- 0.125 #Adjustment parameter in the BoE Taylor rule ##Set to generate observed early period baseline behavior
  alpha_rboe <<- 0.9091641 #Parameter relating to inflation in the BoE Taylor rule ##Set to generate observed early period baseline behavior
  tau_ranfc <<- 0.5 #Adjustment speed of NFC interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  tau_raps <<- 0.5 #Adjustment speed of PS interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  tau_ranmfi <<- 0.5 #Adjustment speed of NMFI interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  tau_ragvt <<- 0.75 #Adjustment speed of GVT interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected to be faster than general rates based on observed past interest rate behavior
  tau_rahh <<- 0.2 #Adjustment speed of HH interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected to be slower than general rates based on observed past interest rate behavior
  tau_rnrow <<- 0.5 #Adjustment speed of RoW interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  tau_rlnfc <<- 0.5271292 #Adjustment speed of NFC interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  tau_rlps <<- 0.5271292 #Adjustment speed of PS interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  tau_rlnmfi <<- 0.5271292 #Adjustment speed of NMFI interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  tau_rlgvt <<- 0.75 #Adjustment speed of GVT interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected to be faster than general rates based on observed past interest rate behavior
  tau_rlhh <<- 0.1391083 #Adjustment speed of HH interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected to be slower than general rates based on observed past interest rate behavior
  delta_rlnfc <<- 0.5 #Short run adjustment speed of NFC interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  delta_rlps <<- 0.5 #Short run adjustment speed of PS interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  delta_rlnmfi <<- 0.5 #Short run adjustment speed of NMFI interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  delta_rlgvt <<- 0.8 #Short run adjustment speed of GVT interest rates to long run rate ##Reasonable number selected from a range of values
  CO_NFF <<- 0.5 #Crowding out coefficient for government green GCF ##Set as a reasonable estimate pending empirical evidence
  alpha_1CRNFC <<-  0 #5.18125 #1-0.0683533 #Parameter in the credit rationing equation for the NFC sector ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_2CRNFC <<- 0.12577    #0.0037118 #Parameter in the credit rationing equation for the NFC sector ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_3CRNFC <<- -5.00531#0.0568916 #Parameter in the credit rationing equation for the NFC sector ##Econometrically estimated
  g_LFB[L] <<- 0.001747 #Initial quarterly labour force growth rate in the baseline - declines to half this value, evolution computed in equations ##Taken from OBR labour market projections
  GDP_ROBR[1:L] <<- GDP_R[1:L] # ##
  HIBAN <<- 0 # ##
  HENBAN <<- 0 # ##
  THIBAN <<- L+T+1 # ##
  THENBAN <<- L+T+1 # ##
  HSELL <<- 0 # ##
  GDP_ADJ[1:L] <<-(ea$GDP[1:L]/1000) # ##
  alpha_0CHHP <<- log(CONS_HHP[L]) - alpha_1CHHP*log(CONS_HHP[L-1]) - alpha_2CHHP*log(YD_HH[L-1]) - alpha_3CHHP*log(FA_HH[L-1]) #Constant parameter in the household consumption equation ##Calculated from Eq. (CONS_HHP)
  K_GVTNFFR[1] <<- 0 # ##
  K_GVTNFF[1] <<- 0 # ##
  IBLTR_GVTNMFIG[L] <<- 0 # ##
  IBL_GVTNMFIG[L] <<- 0 # ##
  OT_IBLGVTNMFIG[L] <<- 0 # ##
  r_IBLGVTNMFIG[L] <<- r_IBLGVT[L] # ##
  sh_rGVTNMFIG <<- 1 # ##
  GREEN_BONDS <<- 0 # ##
  rho_SC <<- 0.2 #Damping parameter for supply-constrained price adjustment (0=no adjustment, 1=full adjustment) ##Selected
  def_max <<- (1+0.2)^(1/4)-1 #Maximum default rate of loans (logistic upper bound) ##selected from a reasonable range of values
  def_1 <<- 8.53 #Parameter of the default rate function ##Taken from DEFINE-GLOBAL
  def_2 <<- 11.05 #Parameter of the default rate function ##Taken from DEFINE-GLOBAL
  alpha_1IMP <<- (1-0.52605)#Lagged dependent variable parameter in the nominal import equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_2IMP <<-  (1-alpha_1IMP)#Lagged domestic demand parameter in the nominal import equation ##Econometrically estimated and rescaled so LR elasticity to DY = 1
  alpha_3IMP <<-  0.16456#Investment share of domestic demand parameter in the nominal import equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_4IMP <<- 0.55222#Relative import price parameter in the nominal import equation ##Econometrically estimated
  beta_2IMP <<- 1.883857 #Proportion of power sector GCF contribution to imports ##Estimated from IO data
  beta_1IMP <<- (GCF[L] - beta_2IMP*GCF_PS[L])/GCF[L] #Proportion of overall GCF contribution to imports ##Estimated from IO data
  delta_1IMP <<-  1.92828#Short-run domestic demand parameter in the nominal import equation ##Econometrically estimated
  RoW_DR[1:L] <<- mydata$RoW_D_B[1:L] #Real RoW Demand ##Taken from global output data
  alpha_1EXP <<- (1-0.27788) #Parameter in the nominal export equation ##Econometrically estimated
  alpha_2EXP <<- 0.27788 #Parameter in the nominal export equation ##Econometrically estimated and rescaled so LR elasticity to world demand = 1
  alpha_3EXP <<- 0.08626 #Parameter in the nominal export equation ##Econometrically estimated
  delta_1EXP <<- 2.04351 #Parameter in the nominal export equation ##Econometrically estimated
  RoW_Dg <<- 1.03^(1/4) #Exogenous Growth of RoW Demand ##Taken as a reasonable estimate
  RoW_Pg[L] <<- 1.03^(1/4) #Exogenous initial Growth of RoW Prices (1.03% p.a.) - declines gradually to 1.025% p.a., evolution computed in equations ##Taken as a reasonable estimate
  beta_DIVNRoW <<- 0.003490421 #Net dividend payment rate to the RoW sector ##Taken as a reasonable estimate based on past values
  #alpha_0WS, alpha_1WS now defined in ECM block (lines 1548-1551)

  
  ###################
  #Model-constrained 
  ###################
  g_ETSB <<- 0.0001283193 #Growth rate of ETS carbon price in the baseline ##Set based on reasonable projections
   alpha_0CRNFC <<- log(CR_NFC[L]/(1-CR_NFC[L])) - alpha_1CRNFC*mean(CR_NFC[(L-20):L]) + alpha_2CRNFC*mean(DSR_NFC[(L-20):L]) - alpha_3CRNFC*mean(FL_MFI[(L-20):L]/FA_MFI[(L-20):L])  #Parameter in the NFC credit rationing equation ##Calculated from Eq. (CR_NFC)
  alpha_0CRPS <<- log(CR_PS[L]/(1-CR_PS[L])) - alpha_1CRNFC*mean(CR_PS[(L-20):L]) + alpha_2CRNFC*mean(DSR_PS[(L-20):L]) - alpha_3CRNFC*mean(FL_MFI[(L-20):L]/FA_MFI[(L-20):L]) #Parameter in the power sector credit rationing equation ##Calculated from Eq. (CR_PS)
  alpha_0IMP <<- log(IMP[L]) - alpha_1IMP*log(IMP[L-1]) - alpha_2IMP*log(DY[L-1]) - alpha_3IMP*log((beta_1IMP*GCF[L]+beta_2IMP*GCF_PS[L])/DY[L]) - alpha_4IMP*log(P_I[L]/P_P[L]) - delta_1IMP*diff(log(DY[(L-1):L]))#Parameter in the nominal import equation ##Calculated from Eq. (IMP)
  alpha_0EXP <<- (log(EXP[L]) - alpha_1EXP*log(EXP[L-1]) - alpha_2EXP*log(RoW_DR[L-1]*P_F[L-1]) - alpha_3EXP*log(REER[L-1]) - delta_1EXP*diff(log(RoW_DR[(L-1):L]*P_F[(L-1):L])))#Parameter in the nominal export equation ##Calculated from Eq. (EXP) to match initial condition
  def_0NFC <<- (def_max - DEF_NFC[L]) / (DEF_NFC[L] * exp(def_1 - def_2 * mean(ILLIQ_NFC[(L-21):(L-1)])))#Parameter of the default rate function for NFCs ##Calculated from Eq. (DEF_NFC)
  def_0PS <<- (def_max - DEF_PS[L]) / (DEF_PS[L] * exp(def_1 - def_2 * mean(ILLIQ_PS[(L-21):(L-1)])))#Parameter of the default rate function for NFCs ##Calculated from Eq. (DEF_NFC)
  E_P[1:L] <<- E[1:L]-E_H[1:L] #Total final energy use from production (TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (E)
  epsilon[1:L] <<- E_P[1:L]/GO_PR[1:L] #Energy intensity of production (TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (E_P)
  E_NELECH[1:L] <<-  E_H[1:L]-E_ELECH[1:L] #Non-electric energy use in housing (TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (E_H)
  E_ELECP[1:L] <<- E_ELEC[1:L] - E_ELECH[1:L] #Electric energy use in production (TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (E_ELEC)
  CF_NFF[1:L] <<-  E_ELECNFF[1:L]/K_PSNFFR[1:L] #Capacity factor of non-fossil fuel electricity production##Calculated from Eq. (E_ELECNFF)
  E_ELECFF[1:L] <<- E_ELEC[1:L] - E_ELECNFF[1:L] #Total final electricity use from fossil fuel sources (TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (E_ELECFF)
  theta_P[1:L] <<- E_ELECP[1:L]/E_P[1:L] #Share of electric energy for production in total energy for production (TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (E_ELECP)
  E_NELEC[1:L] <<- E_NELECP[1:L] + E_NELECH[1:L] #Total final non-electric energy use (TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (E_NELEC)
  E_NELECP[1:L] <<- (1-theta_P[1:L])*E_P[1:L] #Total final production non-electric energy use (TwH)##Calculated  using Eq. (E_NELECP)
  theta_HEN <<- (E_NELECH[L] / (H_EN[L] * (1 - beta_ELECH))) - (theta_HI * H_I[L]) / H_EN[L]#Energy intensity of efficient non-electric housing stock##Calculated from Eq. (E_NELECH)
  theta_HEE <<- ( E_ELECH[L]- (theta_HI * H_I[L] + theta_HEN * H_EN[L]) * beta_ELECH) / H_EE[L]#Energy intensity of efficient electric housing stock##Calculated from Eq. (E_ELECH)
  EMIS_NELEC[1:L] <<- EMIS[1:L] - EMIS_ELEC[1:L] #Total greenhouse gas emissions from non-electric energy (MtCO2e)##Calculated from Eq. (EMIS)
  omega_NELEC[1:L] <<- EMIS_NELEC[1:L]/E_NELEC[1:L] #Emission intensity of non-electric energy production (MtCO2e/TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (EMIS_NELEC)
  omega_ELEC[1:L] <<- EMIS_ELEC[1:L]/E_ELECFF[1:L] #Emission intensity of fossil fuel electric energy production (MtCO2e/TwH)##Calculated from Eq. (EMIS_ELEC)
  pi_1 <<- ((epsilon_max-epsilon_min)/(epsilon[L]-epsilon_min) - 1) / (exp(kappa_1*(K_PG[L-1]/K_PC[L-1])) - (epsilon_max-epsilon_min)/(epsilon[L]-epsilon_min))#Parameter linking green to conventional non-electric capital ratio with the energy intensity of production##Calculated from Eq. (epsilon)
  pi_2 <<- ((CF_max-CF_min)/(CF_NFF[L]-CF_min) - 1) / (exp(kappa_2*(E_ELECNFF[L]/E_ELECFF[L])) - (CF_max-CF_min)/(CF_NFF[L]-CF_min))#Parameter linking non-fossil fuel to fossil fuel electricity generation ratio with the capacity factor of non-fossil fuel electricity generation##Calibrated such that CF_NFF[L+1]=CF_NFF[L]
  pi_3 <<- theta_P[L] / (exp(kappa_3*(K_PG[L-1]/K_PC[L-1]))*(1-theta_P[L]) - 1)#Parameter linking green to conventional non-electric capital ratio with the ratio between electric and non-electric energy use in production##Calculated from Eq. (theta_P)
  pi_4 <<- ((omega_nemax-omega_nemin)/(omega_NELEC[L]-omega_nemin) - 1) / (exp(kappa_4*(K_PG[L-1]/K_PC[L-1])) - (omega_nemax-omega_nemin)/(omega_NELEC[L]-omega_nemin))#Parameter linking green to conventional non-electric capital ratio with the emission intensity of non-electric energy##Calculated from Eq. (omega)
  pi_5 <<- ((omega_emax-omega_emin)/(omega_ELEC[L]-omega_emin) - 1) / (exp(kappa_5*(E_ELECNFF[L-1]/E_ELECFF[L-1])) - (omega_emax-omega_emin)/(omega_ELEC[L]-omega_emin))#Parameter linking green to conventional non-electric capital ratio with the emission intensity of fossil fuel electricity production##Calculated from Eq. (omega)
  GDP[1:L] <<- CONS[1:L]+GCF[1:L]+EXP[1:L]-IMP[1:L]#Gross domestic product (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GDP)
  CONS[1:L] <<- CONS_GVT[1:L] + CONS_HH[1:L]#Consumption (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (CONS)
  GCF[1:L] <<- GCF_NFC[1:L] + GCF_PS[1:L] + GCF_GVT[1:L] + GCF_HH[1:L]#Gross fixed capital formation (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF)
  GDP_R[1:L] <<- CONS_R[1:L]+GCF_R[1:L]+EXP_R[1:L]-IMP_R[1:L]#Real gross domestic product 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GDP_R)
  CONS_R[1:L] <<- CONS[1:L]/(ea$P_CONS[1:L]/100)#Real Consumption 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (CONS_R) and consumption deflators
  GCF_R[1:L] <<- GCF[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Real gross fixed capital formation 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_R) and GFCF deflators
  P[1:L] <<- GDP[1:L]/GDP_R[1:L]#GDP price deflator indexed at Q4 2022##Calculated from Eq. (P)
  GDP_RFK[1:L] <<- GDP_R[1:L]/u[1:L]#Capital-determined potential GDP (£ billion)##Calculated using Eq. (u)
  GDP_RFE[1:L] <<- GDP_R[1:L]/re[1:L]#Labour-determined potential GDP (£ billion)##Calculated using Eq. (re)
  GDP_RMAX[1:L] <<- pmin(GDP_RFK[1:L],GDP_RFE[1:L])#Maximum real supply constrained GDP (£ billion)##Calculated using Eq. (GDP_RMAX)
  lambda[1:L] <<- GDP_RFE[1:L]/LF[1:L] #Labour productivity rate - GDP per employee ##Calculated using Eq. (GDP_RFE)
  nu[1:L] <<- GDP_RFK[1:L]/K_PR[1:L]#Capital productivity rate##Calculated from Eq. (GDP_RFK)
  ITAX_NELEC[1:L] <<-  COV_ETSP[1:L]*P_ETS[1:L]*EMIS_NELEC[1:L] #ETS tax on non-electric energy production ##Calculated from Eq. (ITAX_NELEC)
  EMP_PRI[1:L] <<- EMP[1:L] - EMP_PUB[1:L]#Total employed people in the private sector (millions)##Calculated from Eq. (EMP)
  W_PRI[1:L] <<- W[1:L] - W_PUB[1:L]#Total private wages (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (W)
  GOS_P[1:L] <<- GO_P[1:L]-COST_P[1:L]#Gross operating surplus from production (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GOS_P)
  F_P[1:L] <<- CONS_HHP[1:L] + CONS_GVT[1:L] + GCF[1:L] + EXP[1:L]#Final demand for production sector products ##Calculated from EQ. (F_P)
  COST_P[1:L] <<- W[1:L] +  ITAX_P[1:L] + IC_PP[1:L] + IC_PSP[1:L] +IMP[1:L] #Total "cost" in production module (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (COST_P)
  COST_PS[1:L] <<- ITAX_PS[1:L] + IC_PPS[1:L] + IC_PSPS[1:L] #Total "cost" in power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (COST_PS)
  K_P[1:L] <<- K_NFC[1:L]+K_GVT[1:L]#Total production capital (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_P)
  K_PG[1:L] <<- K_NFCG[1:L]+K_GVTG[1:L]#Total green production capital (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_PG)
  K_PC[1:L] <<- K_NFCC[1:L]+K_GVTC[1:L]#Total conventional production capital (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_PC)
  K_PR[1:L] <<- K_NFCR[1:L]+K_GVTR[1:L]#Real total production capital 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_PR)
  K_PGR[1:L] <<- K_NFCGR[1:L]+K_GVTGR[1:L]#Real total green production capital 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_PGR)
  K_PCR[1:L] <<- K_NFCCR[1:L]+K_GVTCR[1:L]#Real total conventional production capital 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_PCR)
  alpha_PP[1:L] <<- IC_PPR[1:L]/GO_PR[1:L] #Technical coefficient for internal intermediate consumption of the production sector (real terms) ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_PPR)
  alpha_PSP[1:L] <<- epsilon[1:L]*theta_P[1:L] #Technical coefficient for the production sector intermediate consumption of the power products ##Calculated from Eq. (alpha_PSP)
  alpha_PSP[1:L]/((P_ELEC[1:L]/P_P[1:L])*theta_P[1:L]*epsilon[1:L]) #Technical coefficient for the production sector intermediate consumption of the power products in 2022 prices ##Calculated from Eq. (alpha_PSP)
  alpha_PSPS[1:L] <<- IC_PSPS[1:L]/GO_PS[1:L] #Technical coefficient for internal intermediate consumption of the power sector ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_PSPS)
  alpha_PPS[1:L] <<- alpha_FUELPS[1:L] + alpha_OPPS[1:L] #Technical coefficient for the power sector intermediate consumption of production products ##Calculated from Eq. (alpha_PPS)
  alpha_OPPS[1:L] <<- IC_OPPSR[1:L]/GO_PSR[1:L] #Technical coefficient for the power sector intermediate consumption of other production products ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_OPPS)
  alpha_FUELPS[1:L] <<- IC_FUELPSR[1:L]/GO_PSR[1:L] #Technical coefficient for the power sector intermediate consumption of fuel  products ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_FUELPS)
  CONS_HHP[1:L] <<- CONS_HH[1:L] - CONS_HHPS[1:L] #Household consumption (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (CONS_HH)
  F_PS[1:L] <<- CONS_HHPS[1:L] #Final demand of the power sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (F_PS)
  P_ELEC[1:L] <<- ((mydata$INTCONS_PS_PROD[1:L]+mydata$CONS_PS_HH[1:L])/1000)/(E_ELEC[1:L])   #Price of  electricity (£billion/TwH)##Calculated by dividing implied prices to households and production and taking the average
  CONS_HHR[1:L] <<- CONS_HH[1:L]/(ea$P_CONS[1:L]/100) #Household consumption 2022 prices (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (CONS_HHR) 
  CONS_HHPR[1:L] <<- CONS_HHP[1:L]/(ea$P_CONS[1:L]/100) #Household consumption from production 2022 prices (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (CONS_HHPR) 
  CONS_GVTR[1:L] <<- CONS_GVT[1:L]/(ea$P_CONS[1:L]/100) #Government consumption 2022 prices (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (CONS_GVTPR) 
  EXP_R[1:L] <<- EXP[1:L]/(ea$P_EXP[1:L]/100) #Total exports 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (EXP_R) 
  IMP_R[1:L] <<- IMP[1:L]/(ea$P_IMP[1:L]/100) #Total imports 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IMP_R) 
  F_PR[1:L] <<- CONS_HHPR[1:L] + CONS_GVTR[1:L] + GCF_R[1:L] +EXP_R[1:L]#Final demand of the production sector £ billion) (IMP_R)  ##Calculated from Eq. (F_PR)
  P_P[1:L] <<- (CONS_HHP[1:L] + CONS_GVT[1:L] + GCF[1:L] + EXP[1:L])/(CONS_HHPR[1:L] + CONS_GVTR[1:L] + GCF_R[1:L] + EXP_R[1:L])#Production sector price level ##Calculated
  MU[1:L] <<-  pmax(c((P_P[2:(L)])/(UC[1:(L-1)]),(mean(MU[(L-41):(L-1)])+1)) - 1, 0.001) #Mark up over unit costs for the domestic production module ## Calculated from Eq. (P_P), floored at 0.001 for log stability
  alpha_1MU <<- mean(c((MU[45:88]/u[45:88]),(MU[109:132]/u[109:132]))) #Linear relationship between production price mark-up and unit costs ## Calibrated so LR markup at initial utilisation equals mean of 45:88 and 109:132 (excl. COVID and GFC)
    GO_PR[1:L] <<- GO_P[1:L]/P_P[1:L]#Production sector gross output 2022 prices (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (GO_PR)
  GO_PSR[1:L] <<- ((GO_PS[1:L])/P_ELEC[1:L])#Power sector gross output 2022 prices (£ billion) ## Calculated from (GO_PSR)
  IC_OPPS[1:L] <<- IC_PPS[1:L] - IC_FUELPS[1:L]#Intermediate consumption of the power sector for other production products (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_PPS)
  det_IA[1:L] <<- (1-alpha_PP[1:L])*(1-alpha_PSPS[1:L])-(-alpha_PPS[1:L])*(-alpha_PSP[1:L])#Determinant of I - A matrix for calculating Leiontief matrix coefficients##Calculated from Eq. (det_IA)
  L_PP[1:L] <<- (1-alpha_PSPS[1:L])/det_IA[1:L]#Leiontief coefficient for internal intermediate consumption of the production sector  ##Calculated from Eq. (L_PP)
  L_PPS[1:L] <<- alpha_PPS[1:L]/det_IA[1:L]#Leiontief coefficient for the powersector intermediate consumption of the production products ##Calculated from Eq. (L_PPS)
  L_PSP[1:L] <<- alpha_PSP[1:L]/det_IA[1:L]#Leiontief coefficient for the production sector intermediate consumption of the power products ##Calculated from Eq. (L_PSP)
  L_PSPS[1:L] <<- (1-alpha_PP[1:L])/det_IA[1:L]#Leiontief coefficient for internal intermediate consumption of the power sector  ##Calculated from Eq. (L_PSPS)
  GO_P[1:L] <<- F_P[1:L] + IC_PP[1:L] + IC_PPS[1:L] #Gross output from production (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GO_P)
  GO_PS[1:L] <<- F_PS[1:L] + IC_PSPS[1:L] + IC_PSP[1:L] #Gross output from the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GO_P)
  P_FUEL[1:L] <<- ((IC_FUELPS[1:L])/((E_ELECFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L])*GO_PSR[1:L]))/((IC_FUELPS[L])/((E_ELECFF[L]/E_ELEC[L])*GO_PSR[L])) #Price of fuel inputs to the power sector ##Calculated from Eq. (alpha_FUELPS)
  GOS_PS[1:L] <<- (GO_PS[1:L])-(IC_PPS[1:L]+IC_PSPS[1:L]+ITAX_PS[1:L])#Gross operating surplus of the power sector (£ billion)  ##Calculated Eq. (GOS_PS)
  YD_PS[1:L] <<- GOS_PS[1:L] + INTR_PS[1:L] - INTP_PS[1:L]#Disposable income of the power sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YD_PS)
  RP_PS[1:L] <<- YD_PS[1:L] + DIVR_PS[1:L] - DIVP_PS[1:L]#Retained profits of the power sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (RP_PS)
  K_PSFFR[1:L] <<- K_PSR[1:L] - K_PSNFFR[1:L] #Power sector fossil fuel capital 2022 prices (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (K_PSR)
  K_PSFF[1:L] <<- K_PSFFR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100) #Power sector fossil fuel capital (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (K_PSFF)
  K_PSNFF[1:L] <<- K_PSNFFR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100) #Power sector non-fossil fuel capital (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_PSFF)
  GCF_PSFFR[1:L] <<- c(NaN,K_PSFFR[2:L] - ((1-delta_KPSFF[2:L])*K_PSFFR[1:(L-1)])) #Gross capital formation of fossil fuel power capital 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_PSFFR)
  GCF_PSNFFR[1:L] <<- c(NaN,K_PSNFFR[2:L] - ((1-delta_KPSNFF[2:L])*K_PSNFFR[1:(L-1)])) #Gross capital formation of non-fossil fuel power capital 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (K_PSNFFR)
  GCF_PSR[1:L] <<- GCF_PSFFR[1:L] + GCF_PSNFFR[1:L] #Gross capital formation of the power sector 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PSR)
  GCF_PSFF[1:L] <<- GCF_PSFFR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100) #Gross capital formation of fossil fuel power capital (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PSFF)
  GCF_PSNFF[1:L] <<- GCF_PSNFFR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100) #Gross capital formation of non-fossil fuel power capital (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PSNFF)
  GCF_PS[38:L] <<- GCF_PSFF[38:L] + GCF_PSNFF[38:L]#Gross capital formation of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PS)
  LEND_PS[1:L] <<- RP_PS[1:L] - GCF_PS[1:L]#Net-lending position of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_PS)
  OT_IBAPS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBA_PS[2:L] - IBA_PS[1:(L-1)] - IBATR_PS[2:L])#Revaluations of interest bearing assets of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBAPS)
  OT_IBLPS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBL_PS[2:L] - IBL_PS[1:(L-1)] - IBLTR_PS[2:L])#Revaluations of interest bearing liabilities of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBLPS)
  OT_EQAPS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQA_PS[2:L] - EQA_PS[1:(L-1)] - EQATR_PS[2:L])#Revaluations of equity assets of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQAPS)
  OT_EQLPS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQL_PS[2:L] - EQL_PS[1:(L-1)] - EQLTR_PS[2:L])#Revaluations of equity liabilities of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQLPS)
  FA_PS[1:L] <<- IBA_PS[1:L] + EQA_PS[1:L]#Financial assets of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FA_PS)
  FL_PS[1:L] <<- IBL_PS[1:L] + EQL_PS[1:L]#Financial liabilities of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FL_PS)
  FNW_PSM[1:L] <<- FA_PS[1:L] - FL_PS[1:L]#Model determined financial net-worth of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_PSM)
  RES_PS[1:L] <<- FNW_PS[1:L] - FNW_PSM[1:L]#Residual financial instrument of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_PS)
  NW_PS[1:L] <<- FNW_PS[1:L] + K_PS[1:L] #Net worth of the power sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (NW_PS)
  LEV_PS[1:L] <<- IBL_PS[1:L]/K_PS[1:L] #Leverage ratio of the power sector##Calculated from Eq. (LEV_PS)
  YP_NFC[1:L] <<- GOS_P[1:L] + INTR_NFC[1:L] - INTP_NFC[1:L]#NFC income from production (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YP_NFC)
  INTAXR_NFC[1:L] <<-INTAX_NFC[1:L]/W[1:L] #Income tax rate of NFCs##Calculated from Eq. (INTAX_NFC)
  GO_R[1:L] <<- GO[1:L]/P_P[1:L] #Total output (£ billion 2022 prices) ## Calculated from Eq. (GO_R)
  YD_NFC[1:L] <<- YP_NFC[1:L] - INTAX_NFC[1:L]#NFC disposable income (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YD_NFC)
  RP_NFC[1:L] <<- YD_NFC[1:L] + DIVR_NFC[1:L] - DIVP_NFC[1:L]#Retained profits of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (RP_PS)
  GCF_NFCC[1:L] <<- GCF_NFC[1:L] - GCF_NFCG[1:L]#Conventional gross capital formation of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_NFCC)
  GCF_NFCR[1:L] <<- GCF_NFC[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Gross capital formation of the NFC sector 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_NFCR)
  GCF_NFCGR[1:L] <<- GCF_NFCG[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Green gross capital formation of the NFC sector 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_NFCGR)
  GCF_NFCCR[1:L] <<- GCF_NFCC[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Conventional gross capital formation of the NFC sector 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_NFCCR)
  LEND_NFCM[1:L] <<- RP_NFC[1:L] - GCF_NFC[1:L]#Model determined net-lending position of the NFC sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_NFCM)
  DISC_NFC[1:L] <<- LEND_NFC[1:L] - LEND_NFCM[1:L]#Lending discrepency for the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_NFC)
  OT_IBANFC[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBA_NFC[2:L] - IBA_NFC[1:(L-1)] - IBATR_NFC[2:L])#Revaluations of interest bearing assets of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBANFC)
  OT_IBLNFC[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBL_NFC[2:L] - IBL_NFC[1:(L-1)] - IBLTR_NFC[2:L])#Revaluations of interest bearing liabilities of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBLNFC)
  OT_EQANFC[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQA_NFC[2:L] - EQA_NFC[1:(L-1)] - EQATR_NFC[2:L])#Revaluations of equity assets of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQANFC)
  OT_EQLNFC[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQL_NFC[2:L] - EQL_NFC[1:(L-1)] - EQLTR_NFC[2:L])#Revaluations of equity liabilities of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQLNFC)
  FA_NFC[1:L] <<- IBA_NFC[1:L] + EQA_NFC[1:L]#Financial assets of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FA_NFC)
  FL_NFC[1:L] <<- IBL_NFC[1:L] + EQL_NFC[1:L]#Financial liabilities of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FL_NFC)
  FNW_NFCM[1:L] <<- FA_NFC[1:L] - FL_NFC[1:L]#Model determined financial net-worth of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_NFCM)
  RES_NFC[1:L] <<- FNW_NFC[1:L] - FNW_NFCM[1:L]#Residual financial instrument of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_NFC)
  RESTR_NFC[1:L] <<- (IBLTR_NFC[1:L] + LEND_NFC[1:L] + EQLTR_NFC[1:L]) - (IBATR_NFC[1:L] + EQATR_NFC[1:L])  #Residual financial instrument transfer of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBLTR_NFC)
  RESTR_PS[1:L] <<- (IBLTR_PS[1:L] + LEND_PS[1:L] + EQLTR_PS[1:L]) - (IBATR_PS[1:L] + EQATR_PS[1:L])  #Residual financial instrument transfer of the PS sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBLTR_PS)
  OT_RESNFC[1:L] <<-  c(NaN,RES_NFC[2:L] - RES_NFC[1:(L-1)] - RESTR_NFC[2:L])#Revaluations of residual financial instrument of the NFC sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (RES_NFC)
  OT_RESPS[1:L] <<-  c(NaN,RES_PS[2:L] - RES_PS[1:(L-1)] - RESTR_PS[2:L])#Revaluations of residual financial instrument of the power sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (RES_PS)
  OT_RESRoW[1:L] <<-  c(NaN,RES_RoW[2:L] - RES_RoW[1:(L-1)] - RESTR_RoW[2:L])#Revaluations of residual financial instrument of the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (RES_RoW)
  beta_nfc[1:L] <<- GCF_NFCG[1:L]/GCF_NFC[1:L]#Proportion of NFC green investment ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_NFCG)
  K_NFCCR[1:L] <<- K_NFCR[1:L] - K_NFCGR[1:L]#Conventional NFC capital stock 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_NFCR)
  K_NFCC[1:L] <<- K_NFCCR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Conventional NFC capital(£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_NFCC)
  K_NFCG[1:L] <<- K_NFCGR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Green NFC capital(£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_NFCG)
  K_NFC[1:L] <<- K_NFCR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#NFC capital(£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_NFC)
  NW_NFC[1:L] <<- FNW_NFC[1:L] + K_NFC[1:L]#NFC net worth (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (NW_NFC)
  LEV_NFC[1:L] <<- IBL_NFC[1:L]/K_NFC[1:L] #Leverage ratio of the NFC sector##Calculated from Eq. (LEV_NFC)
  LEND_MFIM[1:L] <<- INTR_MFI[1:L] - INTP_MFI[1:L] #Model determined net lending of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_MFIM)
  INTR_MFI[1:L] <<- INTP_NFC[1:L] + INTP_PS[1:L] + INTP_NMFI[1:L] + INTP_GVT[1:L]  + INTP_HH[1:L] #Interest received by the MFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (INTR_MFI)
  INTP_MFI[1:L] <<-  INTR_NFC[1:L] + INTR_PS[1:L] + INTR_NMFI[1:L] + INTR_GVT[1:L] + INTR_HH[1:L]+ INTN_RoW[1:L] #Interest paid by the MFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (INTP_MFI)
  FA_MFI[1:L] <<- IBL_NFC[1:L] + IBL_PS[1:L] + IBL_NMFI[1:L] + IBL_HH[1:L] + IBL_GVTMFI[1:L] #Financial assets of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FA_MFI)
  FL_MFI[1:L] <<- IBA_NFC[1:L] + IBA_PS[1:L] + IBA_NMFI[1:L] + IBA_GVT[1:L] + IBA_HH[1:L] + IBN_RoW[1:L] #Financial liabilities of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FL_MFI)
  FNW_MFIM[1:L] <<- FA_MFI[1:L] - FL_MFI[1:L] #Model determined financial net-worth of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_MFIM)
  RES_MFI[1:L] <<- - (RES_HH[1:L] + RES_NFC[1:L] + RES_NMFI[1:L] + RES_PS[1:L] + RES_GVT[1:L] + RES_RoW[1:L]) #Residual financial instrument of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_MFI)
  DIVR_NMFI[1:L] <<- DIVP_NFC[1:L] + DIVP_PS[1:L] -DIVN_RoW[1:L] #Dividends received by the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (DIVR_NMFI)
  DIVP_NMFI[1:L] <<- DIVR_NFC[1:L] + DIVR_PS[1:L] + DIVR_HH[1:L] #Dividends paid by the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (DIVR_NMFI)
  YD_NMFI[1:L] <<- (INTR_NMFI[1:L] + DIVR_NMFI[1:L]) - (INTP_NMFI[1:L]+DIVP_NMFI[1:L])#NMFI disposable income (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YD_NMFI)
  EQA_NMFI[1:L] <<- EQL_NFC[1:L] +EQL_PS[1:L] #Equity assets of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated as the sum of other sector equity liabilities
  EQL_NMFI[1:L] <<- EQA_HH[1:L] + EQA_NFC[1:L] + EQA_PS[1:L] + EQN_RoW[1:L]#Equity liabilities of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated as the sum of other sector equity assets
  FA_NMFI[1:L] <<- IBA_NMFI[1:L] + IBL_GVTNMFI[1:L] + EQA_NMFI[1:L]#Financial assets of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FA_NMFI)
  FL_NMFI[1:L] <<- IBL_NMFI[1:L] + EQL_NMFI[1:L] + PENS[1:L] + INS[1:L]#Financial liabilities of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FL_NMFI)
  EQATR_NMFI[1:L] <<- EQLTR_NFC[1:L] + EQLTR_PS[1:L]#Equity assets transfers of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (EQATR_NMFI)
  EQLTR_NMFI[1:L] <<- EQATR_NFC[1:L] + EQATR_PS[1:L] + EQATR_HH[1:L] + EQNTR_RoW[1:L]#Equity liability transfers of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (EQLTR_NMFI)
  OT_IBANMFI[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBA_NMFI[2:L] - IBA_NMFI[1:(L-1)] - IBATR_NMFI[2:L]) #Revaluations of interest bearing assets of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBANMFI)
  OT_IBLNMFI[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBL_NMFI[2:L] - IBL_NMFI[1:(L-1)] - IBLTR_NMFI[2:L])#Revaluations of interest bearing liabilities of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBLNMFI)
  OT_EQANMFI[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQA_NMFI[2:L] - EQA_NMFI[1:(L-1)] - EQATR_NMFI[2:L])#Revaluations of equity assets of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQANMFI)
  OT_EQLNMFI[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQL_NMFI[2:L] - EQL_NMFI[1:(L-1)] - EQLTR_NMFI[2:L])#Revaluations of equity liabilities of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQLNMFI)
  FNW_NMFIM[1:L] <<- FA_NMFI[1:L] - FL_NMFI[1:L]#Model determined financial net-worth of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_NMFIM)
  RES_NMFI[1:L] <<- FNW_NMFI[1:L] - FNW_NMFIM[1:L]#Residual financial instrument of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_NMFI)
  RESTR_NMFI[1:L] <<- (IBLTR_NMFI[1:L] + LEND_NMFI[1:L] + EQLTR_NMFI[1:L] + PENSTR[1:L] + INSTR[1:L]) - (IBATR_NMFI[1:L] + EQATR_NMFI[1:L] + IBLTR_GVTNMFI[1:L]) #Residual financial instrument transfer of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBLTR_NMFI)
  WR_PRI[1:L] <<- W_PRI[1:(L)]/EMP_PRI[1:(L)]#Private sector wage rate (£ thousands) ##Calculated from Eq. (W_PRI)
  WR_PUB[1:L] <<- W_PUB[1:(L)]/EMP_PUB[1:(L)]#Public sector wage rate (£ thousands) ##Calculated from Eq. (W_PUB)
  W_S[1:L] <<- (W[1:L])/(GDP[1:L]) #Wage share of GDP ## Calculated from Eq. (W_S)
  alpha_0WS <<-  mean(log(W_S[(91):128]/(1 - W_S[(91):128])) + alpha_1WS*ru[(91):128]) -0.05 #Constant parameter in the wage share equation ##Calibrated so W_S equation equals observed W_S[L] at t=L
  WR[1:L] <<- W[1:(L)]/EMP[1:(L)] #Overall wage rate (£ thousands) ##Calculated from Eq. (WR)
  INTAXR_HH[1:L] <<-INTAX_HH[1:L]/W[1:L] #Income tax rate of Households##Calculated from Eq. (INTAX_HH)
  YD_GVT[1:L] <<- (ITAX[1:L] + INTAX[1:L] + SOCC_GVT[1:L] + INTR_GVT[1:L]) - (SOCB_GVT[1:L]+INTP_GVT[1:L])#Government disposable income (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YD_GVT)
  GCF_GVTC[1:L] <<- GCF_GVT[1:L] - GCF_GVTG[1:L]#Conventional gross capital formation of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_GVTC)
  GCF_GVTR[1:L] <<- GCF_GVT[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Gross capital formation of the GVT sector 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_GVTR)
  GCF_GVTGR[1:L] <<- GCF_GVTG[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Green gross capital formation of the GVT sector 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_GVTGR)
  GCF_GVTCR[1:L] <<- GCF_GVTC[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Conventional gross capital formation of the GVT sector 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_GVTCR)
  LEND_GVTM[1:L] <<-  YD_GVT[1:L] - (W_PUB[1:L]+OCONS_GVT[1:L]+GCF_GVT[1:L])#Model determined net-lending position of the government sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_GVTM)
  DISC_GVT[1:L] <<- LEND_GVT[1:L] - LEND_GVTM[1:L]#Lending discrepancy for the government sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_GVT)
  beta_gvt[1:L] <<- GCF_GVTG[1:L]/GCF_GVT[1:L]#Proportion of government green investment ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_GVTG)
  OT_IBAGVT[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBA_GVT[2:L] - IBA_GVT[1:(L-1)] - IBATR_GVT[2:L]) #Revaluations of interest bearing assets of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBAGVT)
  OT_IBLGVT[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBL_GVT[2:L] - IBL_GVT[1:(L-1)] -IBLTR_GVT[2:L]) #Revaluations of interest bearing liabilities of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBLGVT)
  FA_GVT[1:L] <<- IBA_GVT[1:L]#Financial assets of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FA_GVT)
  FL_GVT[1:L] <<- IBL_GVT[1:L]#Financial liabilities of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FL_GVT)
  FNW_GVTM[1:L] <<- FA_GVT[1:L] - FL_GVT[1:L]#Model determined financial net-worth of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_GVTM)
  RES_GVT[1:L] <<- FNW_GVT[1:L] - FNW_GVTM[1:L]#Residual financial instrument of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_GVT)
  RESTR_GVT[1:L] <<-  (IBLTR_GVT[1:L] + LEND_GVT[1:L]) -(IBATR_GVT[1:L]) #Residual financial instrument transfer of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBLTR_GVT)
  K_GVTCR[1:L] <<- K_GVTR[1:L] - K_GVTGR[1:L]#Conventional GVT capital stock 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_GVTR)
  K_GVTC[1:L] <<- K_GVTCR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Conventional GVT capital(£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_GVTC)
  K_GVTG[1:L] <<- K_GVTGR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Green GVT capital(£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_GVTG)
  K_GVT[1:L] <<- K_GVTR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#GVT capital(£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (K_GVT)
  NW_GVT[1:L] <<- FNW_GVT[1:L] + K_GVT[1:L]#GVT net worth (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (NW_GVT)
  YP_HH[1:L] <<- W[1:L] + (INTR_HH[1:L]+DIVR_HH[1:L]) - (INTP_HH[1:L]) #Household income from production (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YP_HH)
  YD_HH[1:L] <<- YP_HH[1:L] + (SOCB[1:L]) - (SOCC[1:L]+INTAX_HH[1:L]) #Household disposable income (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YD_HH)
  SOCC[1:L] <<- SOCC_NMFI[1:L] + SOCC_GVT[1:L]#Total social contributions paid by the Household sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (SOCC)
  SOCB[1:L] <<- SOCB_NMFI[1:L] + SOCB_GVT[1:L] #Total social benefits received by the Household sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (SOCB)
  DISC_NMFI[1:L] <<- LEND_NMFI[1:L] - LEND_NMFIM[1:L]#Lending discrepancy for the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_NMFI)
  LEND_NMFIM[1:L] <<- YD_NMFI[1:L] - PENSR[1:L] -INSR[1:L]#Model determined net-lending position of the NMFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_NMFIM)
  SAV_HH[1:L] <<- YD_HH[1:L] - CONS_HH[1:L]#Household savings (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (SAV_HH)
  GCF_HHHI[1:L] <<- GCF_HH[1:L] - GCF_HHNB[1:L]#Household Gross capital formation in home improvement 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_HH)
  GCF_HHR[1:L] <<- GCF_HH[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Gross fixed capital formation in new houses (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_HHR)
  GCF_HHNB[1:L] <<- GCF_HHNBR[1:L]*(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Gross fixed capital formation in new houses 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_HHR)
  GCF_HHHIR[1:L] <<- GCF_HHHI[1:L]/(ea$P_GFCF[1:L]/100)#Gross fixed capital formation in home improvements 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_HHR)
  GCF_HHHIER[1:L] <<- beta_GCFHH[1:L]*GCF_HHHIR[1:L] #Gross fixed capital formation in energy efficient home improvements 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_HHHIER)
  GCF_HHHINR[1:L] <<- GCF_HHHIR[1:L] - GCF_HHHIER[1:L] #Gross fixed capital formation in non-energy home improvements 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_HHHINR)
  GCF_HHHIEER[1:L] <<- GCF_HHHIER[1:L] - GCF_HHHIENR[1:L] #Gross fixed capital formation in electrification home improvements 2022 prices (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_HHHIEER)
  LEND_HHM[1:L] <<- SAV_HH[1:L] - GCF_HH[1:L]  + INSR[1:L] + PENSR[1:L] +PENS_ADJ[1:L] #Model determined net-lending position of the HH sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_HHM)
  DISC_HH[1:L] <<- LEND_HH[1:L] - LEND_HHM[1:L]#Lending discrepancy for the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_HH)
  kappa_hie <<- (GCF_HHHIENR[L]*H_EN[L-1]/GCF_HHHIER[L])/((1-(GCF_HHHIENR[L]/GCF_HHHIER[L]))*H_I[L-1])#Adjusting constant for the balance between energy efficiency and electrification home improvements (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. ( GCF_HHHIENR)
  OT_IBAHH[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBA_HH[2:L] - IBA_HH[1:(L-1)] - IBATR_HH[2:L])#Revaluations of interest bearing assets of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBA_HH)
  OT_IBLHH[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBL_HH[2:L] - IBL_HH[1:(L-1)] - IBLTR_HH[2:L]) #Revaluations of interest bearing liabilities of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBL_HH)
  OT_EQAHH[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQA_HH[2:L] - EQA_HH[1:(L-1)] - EQATR_HH[2:L])#Revaluations of equity assets of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (EQA_HH)
  FA_HH[1:L] <<- IBA_HH[1:L] + EQA_HH[1:L] + PENS[1:L] + INS[1:L]#Financial assets of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FA_HH)
  FL_HH[1:L] <<- IBL_HH[1:L] #Financial liabilities of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FL_HH)
  FNW_HHM[1:L] <<- FA_HH[1:L] - FL_HH[1:L]#Model determined financial net-worth of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_HHM)
  RES_HH[1:L] <<- FNW_HH[1:L] - FNW_HHM[1:L]#Residual financial instrument of the H sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_HH)
  RESTR_HH[1:L] <<- IBLTR_HH[1:L] + LEND_HH[1:L] - (IBATR_HH[1:L] + EQATR_HH[1:L]+PENSTR[1:L]+INSTR[1:L]) #Residual financial instrument transfer of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBATR_HH)
  OT_RESHH[1:L] <<-  c(NaN,RES_HH[2:L] - RES_HH[1:(L-1)] - RESTR_HH[2:L])#Revaluations of residual financial instrument of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (RES_HH)
  OT_RESNMFI[1:L] <<-  c(NaN,RES_NMFI[2:L] - RES_NMFI[1:(L-1)] - RESTR_NMFI[2:L])#Revaluations of residual financial instrument of the NMFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (RES_NMFI)
  OT_RESGVT[1:L] <<-  c(NaN,RES_GVT[2:L] - RES_GVT[1:(L-1)] - RESTR_GVT[2:L])#Revaluations of residual financial instrument of the GVT sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (RES_GVT)
  OT_INS[1:L] <<- c(0,INS[2:L] - INS[1:(L-1)] - INSTR[2:L])#Revaluations of insurance assets of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (INS)
  OT_PENS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,PENS[2:L] - PENS[1:(L-1)] - PENSTR[2:L]) #Revaluations of pension assets of the HH sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (PENS)
  HB[1:L] <<- c(NaN,diff(H[1:L]))#Number of houses built in the UK (millions) ##Calculated as the change in house numbers based on housing data
  alpha_HB <<- mean(HB[41:L]/GCF_HHNBR[41:L]) #Conversion between real investment in new houses and the building of new houses##Calculated from Eq. (HB)
  HVAL[1:L] <<- H[1:L]*P_H[1:L]#Total value of housing stock (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (HVAL)
  NW_HH[1:L] <<- FNW_HH[1:L] + HVAL[1:L]#HH net worth (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (NW_HH)
  YP_RoW[1:L] <<- IMP[1:L] - EXP[1:L]#RoW income from production (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (YP_RoW)
  LEND_RoWM[1:L] <<- YP_RoW[1:L] + (INTN_RoW[1:L]) #Disposable income of the rest of the world (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_RoWM)
  DISC_RoW[1:L] <<- LEND_RoW[1:L] - LEND_RoWM[1:L]#Lending discrepancy for the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_RoW)
  OT_IBARoW[1:L] <<- c(NaN,IBN_RoW[2:L] - IBN_RoW[1:(L-1)] - IBNTR_RoW[2:L])#Revaluations of interest bearing assets of the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_IBARoW)
  OT_EQNRoW[1:L] <<- c(NaN,EQN_RoW[2:L] - EQN_RoW[1:(L-1)] - EQNTR_RoW[2:L])#Revaluations of equity assets of the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQNRoW)
  FNW_RoWM[1:L] <<- IBN_RoW[1:L] + EQN_RoW[1:L] + IBL_GVTRoW[1:L]#Model determined financial net-worth of the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_RoWM)
  RES_RoW[1:L] <<- FNW_RoW[1:L] - FNW_RoWM[1:L]#Residual financial instrument of the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_RoW)
  r_IBANFC[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTR_NFC[2:L]/IBA_NFC[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on NFC interest bearing assets ##Calculated from Eq. (INTR_NFC)
  r_IBAPS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTR_PS[2:L]/IBA_PS[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on PS interest bearing assets ##Calculated from Eq. (INTR_PS)
  r_IBANMFI[1:L] <<- c(NaN,(INTR_NMFI[2:L]-(r_IBLGVT[2:L]*IBL_GVTNMFI[1:(L-1)]))/IBA_NMFI[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on NFC interest bearing assets ##Calculated from Eq. (INTR_NMFI)
  r_IBAGVT[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTR_GVT[2:L]/IBA_GVT[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on GVT interest bearing assets ##Calculated from Eq. (INTR_GVT)
  r_IBAHH[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTR_HH[2:L]/IBA_HH[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on HH interest bearing assets ##Calculated from Eq. (INTR_HH)
  r_IBNRoW[1:L] <<- c(NaN,(INTN_RoW[2:L] -(r_IBLGVT[2:L]*IBL_GVTRoW[1:(L-1)]))/IBN_RoW[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on RoW interest bearing assets ##Calculated from Eq. (INTR_RoW)
  r_IBLNFC[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTP_NFC[2:L]/IBL_NFC[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on NFC interest bearing liabilities ##Calculated from Eq. (INTP_NFC)
  r_IBLPS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTP_PS[2:L]/IBL_PS[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on PS interest bearing liabilities ##Calculated from Eq. (INTP_PS)
  r_IBLNMFI[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTP_NMFI[2:L]/IBL_NMFI[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on NMFI interest bearing liabilities ##Calculated from Eq. (INTP_NMFI)
  r_IBLGVT[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTP_GVT[2:L]/IBL_GVT[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on GVT interest bearing liabilities ##Calculated from Eq. (INTP_GVT)
  r_IBLHH[1:L] <<- c(NaN,INTP_HH[2:L]/IBL_HH[1:(L-1)]) #rate of return on HH interest bearing liabilities ##Calculated from Eq. (INTP_HH)
  theta_socb <<- mean(SOCB_NMFI[133:L]/INS[132:(L-1)])#Payment rate on NMFI insurance stock ##Calculated from Eq. (SOCB_NMFI)
  LEND_M[1:L] <<- LEND_NFCM[1:L]  + LEND_PS[1:L] + LEND_MFIM[1:L] + LEND_NMFIM[1:L] +  LEND_HHM[1:L] + LEND_GVTM[1:L] + LEND_RoWM[1:L] #Overall model determined net lending - should equal 0 by definition ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND_M)
  LEND[1:L] <<- LEND_NFC[1:L] + LEND_PS[1:L] + LEND_MFI[1:L] + LEND_NMFI[1:L] +  LEND_HH[1:L] + LEND_GVT[1:L] + LEND_RoW[1:L] #Overall net lending - should equal 0 by definition ##Calculated from Eq. (LEND)
  FNW_M[1:L] <<- FNW_NFCM[1:L] + FNW_MFIM[1:L] + FNW_NMFIM[1:L] + FNW_PSM[1:L] +  FNW_HHM[1:L] + FNW_GVTM[1:L] + FNW_RoWM[1:L] #Overall model determined financial net worth - should equal 0 by definition ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_M)
  FNW[1:L] <<- FNW_NFC[1:L] + FNW_MFI[1:L] + FNW_NMFI[1:L] + FNW_PS[1:L] +  FNW_HH[1:L] + FNW_GVT[1:L] + FNW_RoW[1:L] #Overall financial net worth - should equal 0 by definition ##Calculated from Eq. (FNW)
  FNW_MFI[1:L] <<-  FNW_MFIM[1:L] + RES_MFI[1:L] #Financial net-worth of the MFI sector (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (FNW_MFI)
  CONS_GVT[1:L] <<- W_PUB[1:L] + OCONS_GVT[1:L] #Government consumption (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (CONS_GVT)
  INTAX[1:L] <<- INTAX_NFC[1:L] + INTAX_HH[1:L] #Total income tax received by the government (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (INTAX)
  ITAX[1:L] <<- ITAX_P[1:L] + ITAX_PS[1:L] #Total indirect tax received by the government (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (ITAX)
  RESTR_RoW[1:L] <<- LEND_RoW[1:L] - (IBLTR_GVTRoW[1:L]+ EQNTR_RoW[1:L]+IBNTR_RoW[1:L])  #Residual financial instrument transfer of the RoW sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBATR_RoW)
  CONS_HHPSR[1:L] <<- CONS_HHPS[1:L]/P_ELEC[1:L]#Household consumption from the power sector 2022 prices (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (CONS_HHPSR)
  DISC_MFI[1:L] <<- -(DISC_NFC[1:L] + DISC_NMFI[1:L] + DISC_GVT[1:L] + DISC_HH[1:L] + DISC_RoW[1:L])#Lending discrepancy for the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (DISC_MFI)
  g[1:L] <<- c(NaN,(GDP[2:L]/GDP[1:(L-1)])) #Nominal GDP growth rate ## Calculated from Eq. (g)
  beta_dhh <<- mean((DIVR_HH[L]/DIVP_NMFI[L])/(EQA_HH[L]/(EQL_NMFI[L]))) #Relationship between household equity holding and dividend distribution ##Calculated from Eq. (DIVR_HH)
  beta_dps <<- mean((DIVR_PS[L]/DIVP_NMFI[L])/(EQA_PS[L]/(EQL_NMFI[L]))) #Relationship between power sector holding and dividend distribution ##Calculated from Eq. (DIVR_PS)
  alpha_IBANFC <<- lm(IBATR_NFC[81:L] ~ 0 + (GO_P[81:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Relationship between NFC IBA transfers and total output from production ##Calculated from Eq. (IBATR_NFC)
  alpha_EQANFC <<- lm(EQATR_NFC[81:L] ~ 0 + (GO_P[81:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Relationship between NFC EQA transfers and total output from production ##Calculated from Eq. (EQATR_NFC)
  alpha_IBAPS <<- lm(IBATR_PS[81:L] ~ 0 + (GO_PS[81:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Relationship between PS IBA transfers and total output from power sector ##Calculated from Eq. (IBATR_PS)
  alpha_EQAPS <<- lm(EQATR_PS[81:L] ~ 0 + (GO_PS[81:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Relationship between PS EQA transfers and total output from production ##Calculated from Eq. (EQATR_PS)
  alpha_IBAGVT <<- lm(IBATR_GVT[81:L] ~ 0 + (GO[81:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Relationship between GVT IBA transfers and total output from production ##Calculated from Eq. (IBATR_GVT)
  alpha_IBANMFI <<- lm(IBATR_NMFI[81:L] ~ 0 + (GO[81:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Relationship between NMFI IBA transfers and total output from production ##Calculated from Eq. (IBATR_NMFI)
  GO[1:L] <<- GO_P[1:L] + GO_PS[1:L] #Total output (£ billion) ## Calculated from Eq. (GO)
  delta_IBAPS <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBAPS[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBA_PS[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of PS IBAs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBAPS)
  delta_IBANFC <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBANFC[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBA_NFC[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of NFC IBAs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBANFC)
  delta_IBANMFI <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBANMFI[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBA_NMFI[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of NMFI IBAs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBANMFI)
  delta_IBAGVT <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBAGVT[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBA_GVT[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of GVT IBAs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBAGVT)
  delta_IBAHH <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBAHH[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBA_HH[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of HH IBAs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBAHH)
  delta_IBARoW <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBARoW[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBN_RoW[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of RoW IBAs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBARoW)
  delta_EQNRoW <<-  if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_EQNRoW[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(EQN_RoW[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of RoW EQAs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_EQNRoW)
