```{r}
  delta_RESHH <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_RESHH[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(RES_HH[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of HH residual financial instrument ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_RESHH)
  delta_RESRoW <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_RESRoW[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(RES_RoW[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of RoW residual financial instrument ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_RESRoW)
  delta_RESNMFI <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_RESNMFI[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(RES_NMFI[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of NMFI residual financial instrument ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_RESNMFI)
  delta_RESNFC <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_RESNFC[41:L]/GO_R[41:L] ~ 0 + lag(RES_NFC[41:(L)]/GO_R[41:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of NFC residual financial instrument ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_RESNFC)
  delta_OTEQLNMFI <<- lm(OT_EQLNMFI[81:L] ~ 0 + (DIVP_NMFI[81:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Revaluation rate of NMFI residual financial instrument ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_EQLNMFI)
  delta_RESPS <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_RESPS[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(RES_PS[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of PS residual financial instrument ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_RESPS)
  delta_RESGVT <<- if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_RESGVT[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(RES_GVT[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of GVT residual financial instrument ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_RESGVT)
  psi_EQNMFI <<- sum(EQATR_NMFI[101:132])/sum(EQLTR_NMFI[101:132])#Ratio determining NMFI EQA transfers based on their liability transfers from other sectors ##Calculated from Eq. (EQATR_NMFI)
  alpha_EQAHH <<- 0.005407014 #Relationship between HH EQA transfers and household equity stock##Calculated from Eq. (EQATR_HH)
  delta_INS <<-  if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_INS[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(INS[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of Insurance ##Calculated from EQ. (INS)
  delta_IBLNFC <<-  if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBLNFC[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBL_NFC[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of NFC IBLs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBLNFC)
  DEF_NFC[1:L] <<- c(NaN,-(OT_IBLNFC[2:(L-1)]/IBL_NFC[1:(L-2)]),0.0005203455) #NFC default rates assumed to be proportional to NFC Revaluations ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBLNFC)
  DEF_PS[1:L] <<- c(NaN,-(OT_IBLPS[2:(L-1)]/IBL_PS[1:(L-2)]),0.0005203455) #PS default rates assumed to be proportional to PS Revaluations ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBLPS)
  delta_IBLNMFI <<-  if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBLNMFI[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBL_NMFI[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of NMFI IBLs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBLNMFI)
  delta_IBLPS <<-  if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBLPS[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBL_PS[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of PS IBLs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBLPS)
  delta_IBLHH <<-  if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBLHH[81:L]/GO[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBL_HH[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of NFC IBLs ##Calculated from EQ. (OT_IBLNFC)
  delta_IBLGVT <<-  if(sum(GO_R[41:L])>0){lm(OT_IBLGVT[81:L] ~ 0 + lag(IBL_GVT[81:(L)],1))$coefficients[[1]]} #Revaluation rate of GVT IBLs ##Calculated from E. (OT_IBLGVT)
  GCF_NFCD[1:L] <<- GCF_NFC[1:L]/(1-CR_NFC[1:L]) #Desired NFC gross capital formation ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_NFC)
  GCF_PSFFD[1:L] <<- GCF_PSFF[1:L]/(1-CR_PS[1:L]) #Desired PS fossil fuel gross capital formation ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PSFF)
  GCF_PSNFFD[1:L] <<- GCF_PSNFF[1:L]/(1-CR_PS[1:L]) #Desired PS non-fossil gross capital formation ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PSNFF)
  GCF_PSD[1:L] <<- GCF_PSFFD[1:L] + GCF_PSNFFD[1:L] #Total desired PS gross capital formation ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PSD)
  r_KNFF[1:L] <<- c(NaN, ifelse((K_PSNFF[1:(L-1)] + K_GVTNFF[1:(L-1)]) > 0, (P_ELEC[2:L]*E_ELECNFF[2:L] - COST_PSNFF[2:L])/(K_PSNFF[1:(L-1)] + K_GVTNFF[1:(L-1)]), 0)) #Return rate on NFF capital ##Calculated from Eq. (r_KNFF)
  r_KFF[1:L] <<- c(NaN, ifelse(K_PSFF[1:(L-1)] > 0, (P_ELEC[2:L]*E_ELECFF[2:L] - COST_PSFF[2:L])/K_PSFF[1:(L-1)], 0)) #Return rate on FF capital ##Calculated from Eq. (r_KFF)
  alpha_1bNFF <<- 10 #Sensitivity of NFF investment share to relative capital returns
  alpha_0bNFF <<- log(0.9/0.1) - alpha_1bNFF*0.085 #Habit persistence constant calibrated such that prop_NFF = 0.9 when r_KNFF - r_KFF = 0.085 ##Calculated from Eq. (prop_NFF)
  prop_NFF[1:L] <<- 1/(1+exp(-(alpha_0bNFF + alpha_1bNFF*(r_KNFF[1:L] - r_KFF[1:L])))) #NFF share of PS investment ##Calculated from Eq. (prop_NFF)
  ru[1:L] <<- 1 - re[1:L] #Rate of unemployment##Calculated from Eq. (ru)
  r_BOEQ[1:L] <<- ((1+r_BOE[1:L])^(1/4))-1 #Quarterly Bank of England interest rate ##Calculated from Eq. (r_BOEQ)
  PENS_ADJ[1:L] <<- SOCC_NMFI[1:L] - SOCB_NMFI[1:L] #Adjustment to pension entitlements as defined within the SNA (£ bn) ##Calculated from Eq. (PENS_ADJ)
  ITAXR_PS[1:L] <<- (ITAX_PS[1:L] - COV_ETSPS[1:L] * P_ETS[1:L] * EMIS_ELEC[1:L]) / GO_PS[1:L] #Indirect tax rate (excluding ETS) of power sector ##Calculated from Eq. (ITAXR_PS)
  ITAXR_P[1:L] <<- (ITAX_P[1:L] - COV_ETSP[1:L] * P_ETS[1:L] * EMIS_NELEC[1:L]) / GO_P[1:L] #Indirect tax rate (excluding ETS) of the production sector ##Calculated from Eq. (ITAXR_P)
  P_NELECT[1:L] <<- P_NELEC[1:L] + ITAX_NELEC[1:L]/E_NELEC[1:L] #Non-electric energy price including energy taxes (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (P_NELECT)
  ITAX_PSFF[1:L] <<- ITAXR_PS[1:L]*GO_PS[1:L]*(E_ELECFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L]) + COV_ETSPS[1:L]*P_ETS[1:L]*EMIS_ELEC[1:L] #Indirect tax of the power sector on fossil fuel electricity production (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (ITAX_PSFF)
  ITAX_PSNFF[1:L] <<- ITAXR_PS[1:L]*GO_PS[1:L]*(E_ELECNFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L]) #Indirect tax of the power sector on non-fossil fuel electricity production (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (ITAX_PSNFF)
  COST_PSNFF[1:L] <<- c(NaN,(ITAX_PSNFF[2:L]+(IC_PSPS[2:L]+IC_OPPS[2:L])*(E_ELECNFF[1:(L-1)]/E_ELEC[1:(L-1)])+delta_KPSNFF[2:L]*K_PSNFF[1:(L-1)])) #Total cost of power sector non-fossil fuel electricity production (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (COST_PSNFF)
  COST_PSFF[1:L] <<- c(NaN,(ITAX_PSFF[2:L]+IC_FUELPS[2:L] + (IC_PSPS[2:L]+IC_OPPS[2:L])*(E_ELECFF[1:(L-1)]/E_ELEC[1:(L-1)])+delta_KPSFF[2:L]*K_PSFF[1:(L-1)])) #Total cost of power sector fossil fuel electricity production (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (COST_PSFF)
  AC_NFF[1:L] <<- COST_PSNFF[1:L]/(GO_PSR[1:L]*(E_ELECNFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L])) #Average cost of power sector non-fossil fuel electricity production (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (AC_NFF)
  AC_PS[2:L] <<- (COST_PS[2:L]+INTP_PS[2:L]+DIVP_PS[2:L]-INTR_PS[2:L]-DIVR_PS[2:L]+delta_KPSFF[2:L]*K_PS[1:(L-1)])/GO_PSR[2:L] #Average cost of power sector electricity production (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (AC_PS)
  AC_FF[1:L] <<- COST_PSFF[1:L]/(GO_PSR[1:L]*(E_ELECFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L])) #Average cost of power sector fossil fuel electricity production (£ billion)##Calculated from Eq. (AC_FF)
  E_ELECMAX[1:L] <<- CF_FF*K_PSFFR[1:L]+CF_NFF[1:L]*K_PSNFFR[1:L] #Maximum electrical energy production (TwH) ##Calculated from Eq. (E_ELECMAX)
  GDP_RFELEC[1:L] <<- (E_ELECMAX[1:L] - E_ELECH[1:L])/(theta_P[1:L]*epsilon[1:L]) * (GDP_R[1:L]/GO_PR[1:L]) #Maximum real GDP based on electricity supply capacity (£bn 2022 prices) ##Calculated from Eq. (GDP_RFELEC)
  SOCCR_GVT[1:L] <<- SOCC_GVT[1:L]/W[1:L] #Social contribution rate on government social contributions ##Calculated from Eq. (SOCC_GVT)
  UC[1:L] <<- COST_P[1:L]/GO_PR[1:L] #Unit costs of the production module ##Calculated from Eq. (UC)
  IC_PSPR[1:L] <<- alpha_PSP[1:L]*GO_PR[1:L] #Real intermediate consumption of the production sector for power sector products (£bn 2022 prices) ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_PSPR)
  IC_FUELPSR[1:L] <<- (IC_FUELPS[1:L]/P_FUEL[1:L]) #Intermediate consumption of the power sector for fuel products (£ bn 2022 prices) ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_FUELPS)
  IC_OPPSR[1:L] <<- (IC_OPPS[1:L]/P_P[1:L]) #Intermediate consumption of the power sector for other products (£ bn 2022 prices) ##Calculated from Eq. (IC_OPPS)
  F_PSR[1:L] <<- CONS_HHPSR[1:L] #Final demand of the power sector £ billion)   ##Calculated from Eq. (F_PSR)
  IBATR_MFI[1:L] <<- IBLTR_NFC[1:L] + IBLTR_PS[1:L] + IBLTR_NMFI[1:L] + IBLTR_GVTMFI[1:L] + IBLTR_HH[1:L] #Interest bearing asset transfers of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBATR_MFI)
  IBLTR_MFI[1:L] <<- IBATR_NFC[1:L] + IBATR_PS[1:L] + IBATR_NMFI[1:L] + IBATR_GVT[1:L] + IBATR_HH[1:L] + IBNTR_RoW[1:L] #Interest bearing liability transfers of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (IBLTR_MFI)
  RESTR_MFI[1:L] <<- -(RESTR_HH[1:L] + RESTR_NFC[1:L] + RESTR_NMFI[1:L]+ RESTR_PS[1:L] + RESTR_GVT[1:L] + RESTR_RoW[1:L]) #residual financial instrument transfers of the MFI sector (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (RESTR_MFI)
  r_IBANFCLR[1:L] <<- pmax(minr_nfc, r_BOEQ[1:L]-spr_nfca) #Long run interest bearing asset interest rates for the NFC sector ##Calculated from Eq. (r_IBANFCLR)
  r_IBAPSLR[1:L] <<- pmax(minr_nfc, r_BOEQ[1:L]-spr_nfca) #Long run interest bearing asset interest rates for the Power sector ##Calculated from Eq. (r_IBAPSLR)
  r_IBANMFILR[1:L] <<- pmax(minr_nmfi, r_BOEQ[1:L]-spr_nmfia) #Long run interest bearing asset interest rates for the NMFI sector ##Calculated from Eq. (r_IBANMFILR)
  r_IBAGVTLR[1:L] <<- pmax(minr_gvt, r_BOEQ[1:L]-spr_gvta) #Long run interest bearing asset interest rates for the GVT sector ##Calculated from Eq. (r_IBAGVTLR)
  r_IBAHHLR[1:L] <<- pmax(minr_hh, r_BOEQ[1:L]-spr_hha) #Long run interest bearing asset interest rates for the household sector ##Calculated from Eq. (r_IBAHHLR)
  r_IBNRoWLR[1:L] <<- pmax(minr_row, r_BOEQ[1:L]-spr_rowa) #Long run net interest bearing asset interest rates for the RoW sector ##Calculated from Eq. (r_IBARoWLR)
  MC_FF[1:L] <<- (IC_FUELPS[1:L]+COV_ETSPS[1:L]*P_ETS[1:L]*EMIS_ELEC[1:L])/E_ELECFF[1:L] #Marginal cost of fossil fuel electricity generation (£ bn/ TwH) ##Calculated from Eq. (MC_FF)
  MC_ELEC[1:L] <<- MC_FF[1:L]*(1-(E_ELECNFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L]))^mu_MCELEC#Marginal cost of overall electricity generation (£ bn/ TwH) ##Calculated from Eq. (MC_ELEC)
  COST_ER[1:L] <<-  (COST_NELEC[1:L]  + ITAX_NELEC[1:L] + CONS_HHPS[1:L] + IC_PSP[1:L])/P_P[1:L] #Total economy energy costs from electric and non-electric sources including taxes (£ billion) ##Calculated from Eq. (COST_ET)
  P_ELECLR[1:L] <<- P_ELEC[1:L] #Long run electricity price (£bn / TwH) ##Set equal to initial electricity price
  P_ELEC_BASE[1:L] <<- P_ELEC[1:L] #Base electricity price before supply constraint adjustment (£bn / TwH) ##Set equal to initial P_ELEC
  IC_PSP_SC[1:L] <<- IC_PSP[1:L] #Supply-constrained intermediate consumption of electricity by production sector (£ billion) ##Set equal to initial IC_PSP
  ILLIQ_NFC[1:(L)] <<- c(((INTP_NFC[1:(L-1)]+INTAX_NFC[1:(L-1)]+DIVP_NFC[1:(L-1)]+GCF_NFC[1:(L-1)]+delta_KP[1:(L-1)]*K_NFC[1:(L-1)]+IBATR_NFC[1:(L-1)]+EQATR_NFC[1:(L-1)])/(GOS_P[1:(L-1)]+INTR_NFC[1:(L-1)]+DIVR_NFC[1:(L-1)]+EQLTR_NFC[1:(L-1)]+IBLTR_NFC[1:(L-1)])),1.18) #Illiquidity ratio of the NFC sector ##Calculated from Eq. (ILLIQ_NFC)
  ILLIQ_PS[1:L] <<- c(((INTP_PS[1:(L-1)]+ITAX_PS[1:(L-1)]+DIVP_PS[1:(L-1)]+GCF_PS[1:(L-1)]+delta_KPSFF[1:(L-1)]*K_PS[1:((L-1))]+IBATR_PS[1:(L-1)]+EQATR_PS[1:(L-1)]+IC_PPS[1:(L-1)]+IC_PSPS[1:(L-1)])/(GO_PS[1:(L-1)]+INTR_PS[1:(L-1)]+DIVR_PS[1:(L-1)]+EQLTR_PS[1:(L-1)]+IBLTR_PS[1:(L-1)]+SUBS_PS[1:(L-1)])),1.0463945)  #Illiquidity ratio of the power sector ##Calculated from Eq. (ILLIQ_PS)
  beta_WRPRI <<- WR_PRI[L]/WR[L] #Parameter linking private sector wage rate to overall economy wage rate ##Calculated from Eq. (WR_PRI)
  beta_WRPUB <<- WR_PUB[L]/WR[(L-1)] #Parameter linking public sector wage rate to overall economy wage rate ##Calculated from Eq. (WR_PUB)
  beta_NFF[1:L] <<- E_ELECNFF[1:L]/E_ELEC[1:L] #Share of non-fossil fuel electrical energy in total electrical energy production ##Calculated from Eq. (beta_NFF)
  alpha_SOCCW <<- ((SOCC_NMFI[L] - PENSR[L])/W[L]) #Rate of wage contribution to household social contribution for pension schemes (Defined contribution) ##Calculated from Eq. (SOCC_NMFI)
  alpha_SOCBPENS <<- SOCB_NMFI[L]/PENS[L-1] #Rate of social benefit payments relative to total value of pension schemes ##Calculated from Eq. (SOCB_NMFI)
  beta_EQLNFC <<- 0.001675657  #Parameter determining NFC equity liability price revaluation rate ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQLNFC)
  beta_EQLPS <<- 0.001675657  #Parameter determining power sector equity liability price revaluation rate ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQLPS)
  beta_EQLNMFI <<- 0.00859335  #Parameter determining NMFI equity liability price revaluation rate ##Calculated from Eq. (OT_EQLNMFI)
  alpha_EQNTRRoW <<- lm(EQNTR_RoW[101:L] ~ 0 + (YP_RoW[101:L]))$coefficients[[1]] #Relationship between net RoW equity transfers and RoW income ##Calculated from Eq. (EQNTR_RoW)
  DSR_NFC[2:L] <<- ((YD_NFC[2:L]-delta_KP[2:L]*K_NFC[1:(L-1)]+INTP_NFC[2:L])/INTP_NFC[2:L])#The debt-service ratio of the NFC sector - defined similarly to the DEFINE global model ##Calculated from Eq. (DSR_NFC)
  DSR_PS[2:L] <<- ((YD_PS[2:L]+INTP_PS[2:L]-delta_KPSFF[2:L]*K_PS[1:(L-1)])/INTP_PS[2:L]) #The debt-service ratio of the power sector - defined similarly to the DEFINE global model ##Calculated from Eq. (DSR_PS)
  u_FF[1:L] <<- E_ELECFF[1:L]/(CF_FF*K_PSFFR[1:L]) # Utilisation of fossil based electricity capital ##Calculated from Eq. (u_FF)
  u_NFF[1:L] <<- E_ELECNFF[1:L]/(CF_NFF[1:L]*K_PSNFFR[1:L]+K_GVTNFFR[1:L]) # Utilisation of non-fossil based electricity capital ##Calculated from Eq. (u_NFF)
  u_PS[1:L] <<- E_ELEC[1:L]/(E_ELECMAX[1:L]) # Utilisation of ovewrall electricity capital ##Calculated from Eq. (u_PS)
  # Matrix creation, seeding, and historical u_W computation - must come before alpha calibration
  delta_PS <<- 0.5^(1/20) #Hyperbolic discount parameter for PS expected utilisation (half-life = 20 quarters / 5 years)
  H_PS <<- 80 #Planning horizon for PS expected utilisation (quarters)
  H_NFC <<- 40 #Planning horizon for NFC expected utilisation (quarters)
  K_PSNFF_E <<- matrix(0, nrow = L+T, ncol = H_PS) #Expected non-fossil capital stock matrix
  K_PSFF_E  <<- matrix(0, nrow = L+T, ncol = H_PS) #Expected fossil capital stock matrix
  E_ELEC_E  <<- matrix(0, nrow = L+T, ncol = H_PS) #Expected total electricity demand matrix
  u_PS_E    <<- matrix(0, nrow = L+T, ncol = H_PS) #Expected PS utilisation matrix
  W_PS <<- delta_PS^(1:H_PS) #Hyperbolic discount weights for PS expected utilisation
  u_NFC_W[1:L] <<- GDP_R[1:L] / (nu[1:L] * K_PR[1:L]) #u_NFC_W = u[t] in base case (no NFC policy)
  K_PSNFF_E[1:L,] <<- K_PSNFFR[1:L] #Current NFF capital, constant across horizon (no policy effect)
  K_PSFF_E[1:L,]  <<- K_PSFFR[1:L] #Current FF capital, constant across horizon (no policy in base case)
  E_ELEC_E[1:L,]  <<- E_ELEC[1:L] #Current electricity demand, constant across horizon
  u_PS_E[1:L,] <<- E_ELEC_E[1:L,] / (CF_FF * K_PSFF_E[1:L,] + CF_NFF[1:L] * (K_PSNFF_E[1:L,] + K_GVTNFFR[1:L])) #Expected PS utilisation (= current in base case)
  u_PS_W[2:L] <<- CRED * (u_PS[2:L] + u_PS_E[2:L,] %*% W_PS) / (1 + sum(W_PS)) + (1-CRED) * u_PS[2:L] #Hyperbolic-discounted weighted expected PS utilisation for 2:L (CRED-blended)
  alpha_0GCFNFC <<- (GCF_NFCD[L]/K_NFC[L-1]) - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_NFC_W[L]-u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*(GCF_NFCD[L-1]/K_NFC[L-2]) #Parameter in the NFC gross capital formation equation ##Calibrated to match initial condition
  alpha_0GCFPS <<- GCF_PSD[L]/K_PS[L-1] - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_PS_W[L]-u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*GCF_PSD[L-1]/K_PS[L-2] #Parameter in the unified power sector investment equation ##Calculated from Eq. (GCF_PSD)
  I_ADJ[1:L] <<- P_I[1:L]/(P_F[1:L]*ER[1:L]) #Import price index adjustment based on changing basket of import goods ##Calculated from Eq. (P_I)
  REER[1:L] <<- (P_E[1:L]/(ER[1:L]*P_F[1:L])) #Real effective exchange rate ##Calculated from Eq. (REER)
  DY[1:L] <<- CONS[1:L] + GCF[1:L] + EXP[1:L] #Domestic outpout##Calculated from Eq. (DY)
  SPEND_GVT[1:L] <<- OCONS_GVT[1:L] + GCF_GVT[1:L] + SPEND_GVTPS[1:L] + SOCB_GVT[1:L]#Total Government spending (excl. wages)##Calculated from Eq. (SPEND_GVT)
}

check_duplicates <- function(code_lines) {
  # Combine the code lines into one text string
  code_text <- paste(code_lines, collapse = "\n")
  
  # Regular expression to match assignments with <<-
  # This captures the variable name (allowing letters, numbers, underscores, and dots)
  # optionally followed by an indexing expression (like [1:L])
  pattern <- "([A-Za-z0-9_.]+)\\s*(\\[[^\\]]*\\])?\\s*<<-"
  
  # Find all matches in the code text
  matches <- gregexpr(pattern, code_text, perl = TRUE)
  assignment_matches <- regmatches(code_text, matches)[[1]]
  
  # Extract variable names from the matched strings (the first capture group)
  var_names <- sapply(assignment_matches, function(x) sub(pattern, "\\1", x))
  
  # Identify unique variable names that are assigned more than once
  duplicates <- unique(var_names[duplicated(var_names)])
  return(duplicates)
}

# Use deparse() to get the code of your function as a character vector
code_lines <- deparse(Parameters_InitialValues)

# Run the duplicate check
dups <- check_duplicates(code_lines)

if (length(dups) > 0) {
  cat("Duplicate assignments found for the following variables:\n")
  print(dups)
  stop("Execution terminated")
} else {
  cat("No duplicate assignments found.\n")
}



```

## Create Symbols and Vectors for Endogenous Variables

```{r}
######################
#List of Greek letters
######################
greek_letters <- c("alpha","beta","gamma","delta","epsilon","tau","eta","theta","iota","kappa","lambda","mu","nu","xi","omicron","pi","rho","sigma","upsilon","phi","chi","psi","omega")
#############################################
#Symbols and vectors for endogenous variables
#############################################
code_body <- body(Equations) 
code_lines <- as.character(code_body)
Symbols_var <- regmatches(code_lines, regexpr("^[^[]+", code_lines)) 
Symbols_var <- sub("\\[t\\] <<-.*", "", Symbols_var)
Symbols_var <- Symbols_var[!Symbols_var %in% c("{", "}") & Symbols_var != ""]
for (v in 1:length(Symbols_var)){assign(noquote(paste(Symbols_var[v])), vector(length=L+T))}
Symbols_var_sort <- sort(Symbols_var)
is_greek <- grepl(paste(greek_letters, collapse = "|"), Symbols_var_sort, ignore.case = TRUE) 
Symbols_var_sort <- c(sort(Symbols_var_sort[!is_greek]), Symbols_var_sort[is_greek])
#######################
#Symbols for parameters
#######################
code_body <- body(Parameters_InitialValues) 
code_lines <- as.character(code_body) 
Symbols_par <- regmatches(code_lines, regexpr("^[^\\[]+<<-", code_lines)) 
Symbols_par <- sub("<<-$", "", Symbols_par) 
Symbols_par <- Symbols_par[Symbols_par != ""]  
Symbols_par_sort <- sort(Symbols_par) 
is_greek <- grepl(paste(greek_letters, collapse = "|"), Symbols_par_sort, ignore.case = TRUE) 
Symbols_par_sort <- c(sort(Symbols_par_sort[!is_greek]), Symbols_par_sort[is_greek])
```

# Running the Model and the Scenarios and Saving the Results

```{r}

calculate_calibration_growth_rates <- function(L, cal_periods) {
  growth_rates <- list()
  for (var in calibration_config$target_variables) {
    var_values <- eval(parse(text = var))
    start_val <- var_values[L+1]  # First simulation period
    end_val <- var_values[L+cal_periods]  # Period L+29
    
    # Total growth over calibration period
    total_growth <- end_val / start_val
    growth_rates[[var]] <- total_growth
  }
  return(growth_rates)
}

# Updated function to handle different targets per variable
adjust_calibration_parameters <- function(deviations, cal_iter) {
  for (i in seq_along(calibration_config$target_variables)) {
    var <- calibration_config$target_variables[i]
    param <- calibration_config$flex_parameters[i]
    
    current_val <- eval(parse(text = param))
    direction <- calibration_config$direction_multipliers[param]
    
    # Get the specific target for this variable
    target <- calibration_config$target_growth_total[[var]]
    
    # Calculate adjustment relative to the specific target
    adjustment <- direction * calibration_config$adjustment_factor * deviations[var] / target
    new_val <- current_val + adjustment
    
    assign(param, new_val, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    
    cat("Iteration", cal_iter, "- Adjusting", param, "from", 
        round(current_val, 6), "to", round(new_val, 6), 
        "| Target:", target, "| Deviation:", round(deviations[var], 6), "\n")
  }
}
check_calibration_targets <- function(growth_rates, config) {
  deviations <- numeric(length(config$target_variables))
  names(deviations) <- config$target_variables
  
  for (var in config$target_variables) {
    target <- if(is.list(config$target_growth_total)) {
      config$target_growth_total[[var]]
    } else {
      config$target_growth_total
    }
    deviations[var] <- growth_rates[[var]] - target
  }
  return(deviations)
}

```

```{r chart_block_fn, include=FALSE}
# ChartBlock: plots scenarios from a single block run into a specified output directory.
# active_scen_cfg : subset of scenario_config for this block (already filtered)
# output_dir      : folder to write the PNG into
ChartBlock <- function(filename, variable, label, ymin = NULL, ymax = NULL,
                       legend_x, legend_y,
                       output_dir,
                       active_scen_cfg,
                       periods  = (L + 13):(L + T),
                       x_breaks = seq((L + 13), (L + T), by = 4),
                       x_labels = seq(Sim_Start + 3, Sim_End - 1, by = 1)) {

  required_params <- c("name", "colour", "linetype", "shape", "size", "include_in_plots")
  lapply(active_scen_cfg, function(sc) {
    missing_params <- setdiff(required_params, names(sc))
    if (length(missing_params) > 0)
      stop(paste("Scenario", sc$name, "missing parameters:", paste(missing_params, collapse = ", ")))
  })

  active_scenarios <- active_scen_cfg[
    vapply(active_scen_cfg, function(x) isTRUE(x$include_in_plots), logical(1))
  ]

  # Put Baseline last so it renders on top
  baseline_index <- which(sapply(active_scenarios, function(x) x$name == "Baseline"))
  if (length(baseline_index) > 0)
    active_scenarios <- c(active_scenarios[-baseline_index], active_scenarios[baseline_index])

  if (length(active_scenarios) == 0)
    stop("No scenarios marked for inclusion in plots (include_in_plots = TRUE)")

  data_list <- lapply(active_scenarios, function(sc) {
    df_name <- paste0("Variables_", sc$name)
    if (!exists(df_name)) stop(paste("Data frame not found:", df_name))
    df <- get(df_name)
    if (!variable %in% colnames(df)) stop(paste("Variable", variable, "not found in", df_name))
    df[periods, variable, drop = FALSE]
  })

  dataframe <- data.frame(Periods = periods, do.call(cbind, data_list))
  colnames(dataframe) <- c("Periods", sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "name"))

  data_long        <- pivot_longer(dataframe, -Periods, names_to = "Scenario", values_to = "Value")
  data_nonbaseline <- subset(data_long, Scenario != "Baseline")
  data_baseline    <- subset(data_long, Scenario == "Baseline")

  # Auto-expand y-axis: specified bounds act as a preferred minimum window but
  # are widened automatically if any scenario result falls outside them.
  val_min  <- min(data_long$Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  val_max  <- max(data_long$Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  ylim_lo  <- if (!is.null(ymin)) min(ymin, val_min) else NULL
  ylim_hi  <- if (!is.null(ymax)) max(ymax, val_max) else NULL

  plot <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = Periods, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(data = data_nonbaseline,
              aes(color = Scenario, linetype = Scenario, size = Scenario)) +
    geom_line(data = data_baseline,
              aes(color = Scenario, linetype = Scenario, size = Scenario)) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Scenario, color = Scenario), size = 1.5, stroke = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = setNames(
      sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "colour"), sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "name"))) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = setNames(
      sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "linetype"), sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "name"))) +
    scale_size_manual(values = setNames(
      sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "size"), sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "name"))) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = setNames(
      sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "shape"), sapply(active_scenarios, `[[`, "name"))) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      text                  = element_text(family = "serif"),
      axis.title            = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black"),
      axis.text             = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
      axis.ticks            = element_line(colour = "black"),
      legend.position       = c(legend_x, legend_y),
      legend.title          = element_blank(),
      legend.text           = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
      legend.key.width      = unit(1, "cm"),
      legend.key.height     = unit(0, "cm"),
      legend.key            = element_blank(),
      legend.background     = element_blank(),
      legend.box.background = element_blank(),
      plot.margin           = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"),
      panel.border          = element_rect(color = "black", size = 0.8),
      panel.grid.major      = element_line(size = 0.1, linetype = "solid", colour = "grey"),
      panel.grid.minor      = element_line(size = 0.1, linetype = "solid", colour = "grey")
    ) +
    labs(y = label, x = "Year") +
    { if (!is.null(ylim_lo) || !is.null(ylim_hi)) coord_cartesian(ylim = c(ylim_lo, ylim_hi)) else NULL } +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, labels = x_labels, expand = c(0, 0))

  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, filename), plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}
```

```{r run_blocks, include=FALSE}
# Helper: snapshot Variables_* tables for one scenario and optionally write CSVs.
save_scenario_tables <- function(sce, out_tables_dir) {
  tn  <- paste0("Variables_", sce$name)
  tns <- paste0("Variables_sorted_", sce$name)
  tnp <- paste0("Parameters_sorted_", sce$name)

  vals <- setNames(data.frame(matrix(ncol = length(Symbols_var), nrow = L + T)), Symbols_var)
  for (v in seq_along(Symbols_var)) vals[, v] <- eval(parse(text = Symbols_var[v]))
  assign(tn, signif(vals, 4), envir = .GlobalEnv)

  vals_s <- setNames(data.frame(matrix(ncol = length(Symbols_var_sort), nrow = L + T)), Symbols_var_sort)
  for (v in seq_along(Symbols_var_sort)) vals_s[, v] <- eval(parse(text = Symbols_var_sort[v]))
  assign(tns, signif(vals_s, 4), envir = .GlobalEnv)

  vals_p <- setNames(data.frame(matrix(ncol = length(Symbols_par_sort), nrow = 1)), Symbols_par_sort)
  for (v in seq_along(Symbols_par_sort)) {
    val <- eval(parse(text = Symbols_par_sort[v]))
    vals_p[, v] <- if (length(val) == 1) val else NA
  }
  assign(tnp, signif(vals_p, 4), envir = .GlobalEnv)

  if (result_tables == 1) {
    dir.create(out_tables_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
    write.csv(get(tn),  file.path(out_tables_dir, paste0("Variables_",          sce$name, ".csv")))
    write.csv(get(tns), file.path(out_tables_dir, paste0("Variables-sorted_",   sce$name, ".csv")))
    write.csv(get(tnp), file.path(out_tables_dir, paste0("Parameters-sorted_",  sce$name, ".csv")))
  }
}

# Main block runner: for each result block, optionally loop over credibility cases,
# run only the block's scenarios, save tables, and generate block plots.
#
# Cache: scenarios unaffected by credibility overrides (everything except S2 and S4)
# produce identical results regardless of which block or cred_case they appear in.
# We skip the expensive solve on repeat visits and restore from cache instead.
cred_sensitive_scenarios <- c(2, 4)
scenario_run_cache <- list()  # key -> list(vars, vars_sorted, pars_sorted, gdp_r, g_nom, p_p)

for (block in result_blocks) {

  # A NULL cred_cases entry means a single run with no credibility override
  run_cases <- if (!is.null(block$cred_cases)) block$cred_cases else list(NULL)

  # Filter scenario_config to only this block's scenarios
  block_scen_cfg <- Filter(function(sc) sc$scenario_num %in% block$scenario_nums, scenario_config)

  for (cred_case in run_cases) {

    # Build output paths (case subfolder only when cred_cases is used)
    sub_name       <- if (!is.null(cred_case)) cred_case$name else NULL
    out_plots_dir  <- if (!is.null(sub_name)) file.path(dir_plots,  block$name, sub_name) else file.path(dir_plots,  block$name)
    out_tables_dir <- if (!is.null(sub_name)) file.path(dir_tables, block$name, sub_name) else file.path(dir_tables, block$name)
    dir.create(out_plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

    for (sce in block_scen_cfg) {
      # Cred-insensitive scenarios share results across all blocks and cred_cases;
      # cred-sensitive ones (S2, S4) are keyed by their cred_case name.
      cache_key <- if (sce$scenario_num %in% cred_sensitive_scenarios) {
        paste(sce$scenario_num, if (!is.null(cred_case)) cred_case$name else "none")
      } else {
        as.character(sce$scenario_num)
      }

      if (!is.null(scenario_run_cache[[cache_key]])) {
        # Restore cached results — skip the expensive model solve
        cached <- scenario_run_cache[[cache_key]]
        assign(paste0("Variables_",         sce$name), cached$vars,        envir = .GlobalEnv)
        assign(paste0("Variables_sorted_",  sce$name), cached$vars_sorted,  envir = .GlobalEnv)
        assign(paste0("Parameters_sorted_", sce$name), cached$pars_sorted,  envir = .GlobalEnv)
        assign(paste0("GDP_R_S", sce$scenario_num), cached$gdp_r, envir = .GlobalEnv)
        assign(paste0("G_NOM_S", sce$scenario_num), cached$g_nom, envir = .GlobalEnv)
        assign(paste0("P_P_S",   sce$scenario_num), cached$p_p,   envir = .GlobalEnv)
        if (result_tables == 1) {
          dir.create(out_tables_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
          write.csv(cached$vars,        file.path(out_tables_dir, paste0("Variables_",         sce$name, ".csv")))
          write.csv(cached$vars_sorted, file.path(out_tables_dir, paste0("Variables-sorted_",  sce$name, ".csv")))
          write.csv(cached$pars_sorted, file.path(out_tables_dir, paste0("Parameters-sorted_", sce$name, ".csv")))
        }
      } else {
        # Run the model for this scenario
        for (iterations in 1:5) Parameters_InitialValues()

        for (i in (L + 1):(L + T)) {
          if (i >= sce$start) {
            sce$parameters()
            # Apply credibility override after scenario params — only affects S2 and S4
            if (!is.null(cred_case)) {
              CRED        <<- cred_case$CRED
              GVT_CRED    <<- cred_case$GVT_CRED
              ELEC_BUFFER <<- if (!is.null(cred_case$ELEC_BUFFER)) cred_case$ELEC_BUFFER else 0
            }
          }
          for (iterations in 1:100) Equations(t = i)
        }

        # Store multiplier arrays (keyed by scenario number; last write per scenario wins)
        assign(paste0("GDP_R_S", sce$scenario_num), GDP_R)
        assign(paste0("G_NOM_S", sce$scenario_num), CONS_GVT + GCF_GVT + SPEND_GVTPS)
        assign(paste0("P_P_S",   sce$scenario_num), P_P)

        save_scenario_tables(sce, out_tables_dir)

        # Store in cache for future blocks / cred_cases
        scenario_run_cache[[cache_key]] <- list(
          vars        = get(paste0("Variables_",         sce$name)),
          vars_sorted = get(paste0("Variables_sorted_",  sce$name)),
          pars_sorted = get(paste0("Parameters_sorted_", sce$name)),
          gdp_r       = get(paste0("GDP_R_S", sce$scenario_num)),
          g_nom       = get(paste0("G_NOM_S", sce$scenario_num)),
          p_p         = get(paste0("P_P_S",   sce$scenario_num))
        )
      }
    }

    # Generate plots for this block / credibility case
    if (make_plots == 1) {
      for (cfg in block$chart_configs) {
        ChartBlock(
          filename        = cfg$filename,
          variable        = cfg$variable,
          label           = cfg$label,
          ymin            = cfg$ymin,
          ymax            = cfg$ymax,
          legend_x        = cfg$legend_x,
          legend_y        = cfg$legend_y,
          output_dir      = out_plots_dir,
          active_scen_cfg = block_scen_cfg
        )
      }
    }

  } # end cred_cases loop
} # end result_blocks loop

```


## Fiscal Multiplier
```{r fiscal_multiplier}
sim_idx <- (L+1):(L+T)

# Use baseline price path to deflate G in all scenarios — avoids price-level confounding
P_P_base_sim <- get("P_P_S1")[sim_idx]

# Baseline real G (nominal components deflated by baseline P_P)
G_R_S1   <- get("G_NOM_S1")[sim_idx] / P_P_base_sim
GDP_R_S1 <- get("GDP_R_S1")[sim_idx]

sce_list <- Filter(function(s) s$num %in% Scenarios, list(
  list(num=2, name="Fossil Fuel Ban"),
  list(num=3, name="Power Sector Subsidy"),
  list(num=4, name="FF Ban + Subsidy"),
  list(num=5, name="Housing Regulation"),
  list(num=6, name="Housing Reg + Subsidy"),
  list(num=7, name="Both Regs + Subsidies"),
  list(num=8, name="Green Public Investment"),
  list(num=9, name="GPI + Green Bonds")
))

if (length(sce_list) == 0) {
  message("Fiscal multiplier skipped: no policy scenarios (2-9) in Scenarios.")
} else {

mult_results <- lapply(sce_list, function(s) {
  # Deflate by baseline P_P so delta_G reflects nominal spending change only
  delta_G   <- get(paste0("G_NOM_S", s$num))[sim_idx] / P_P_base_sim - G_R_S1
  delta_GDP <- get(paste0("GDP_R_S", s$num))[sim_idx] - GDP_R_S1

  # Cumulative spending up to each horizon
  cum_G <- cumsum(delta_G)

  # M(h) = ΔY(h) / Σ ΔG(1→h)
  mult <- ifelse(abs(cum_G) > 1e-6, delta_GDP / cum_G, NA)

  data.frame(
    scenario  = s$name,
    quarter   = 1:T,
    delta_G   = delta_G,
    delta_GDP = delta_GDP,
    cum_G     = cum_G,
    mult      = mult
  )
})

# Summary table: M(h) = ΔY(h) / Σ ΔG(1→h)
mult_summary <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(sce_list), function(i) {
  r <- mult_results[[i]]
  # First quarter where cumulative spending diverges from baseline
  first_q <- which(!is.na(r$mult))[1]
  # Multiplier at h quarters after spending starts
  m <- function(h) {
    q <- first_q + h - 1
    if (is.na(first_q) || q > nrow(r)) return(NA)
    round(r$mult[q], 2)
  }
  data.frame(
    Scenario      = sce_list[[i]]$name,
    First_G_Q     = ifelse(is.na(first_q), NA, first_q),
    TotalDeltaG   = round(sum(r$delta_G,   na.rm=TRUE), 2),
    TotalDeltaGDP = round(sum(r$delta_GDP, na.rm=TRUE), 2),
    M_Impact      = m(1),
    M_4Q          = m(4),
    M_8Q          = m(8),
    M_20Q         = m(20),
    M_EndSim      = round(r$mult[T], 2)
  )
}))

print(mult_summary)
write.csv(mult_summary, file.path(dir_tables, "Multiplier_Summary.csv"), row.names=FALSE)

} # end if(length(sce_list) > 0)
```


## Sensitivity Helper Functions
```{r sensitivity_helpers}
if(run_sensitivity == 1) {

  # ---- Core runner --------------------------------------------------------
  # cases:        list of list(name, setup_fn, step_fn=NULL)
  #   setup_fn()  called after Parameters_InitialValues() — use for param overrides
  #   step_fn()   called after sce$parameters() at each sim step — use for flag overrides
  # sce_nums_show: scenario numbers to include in output table
  run_sens_scenarios <- function(cases, vars_info, t_pts, yr_lbs,
                                  sce_nums_show = 1:9) {
    sens_out <- list()
    for (case in cases) {
      sce_out <- list()
      for (sce in scenario_config) {
        for (iterations in 1:5) Parameters_InitialValues()
        case$setup_fn()                          # parameter-level override
        for (i in (L+1):(L+T)) {
          if (i >= sce$start) {
            sce$parameters()
            if (!is.null(case$step_fn)) case$step_fn()  # flag-level override
          }
          for (iterations in 1:100) Equations(t = i)
        }
        if (sce$scenario_num %in% sce_nums_show) {
          row <- lapply(vars_info, function(vi) round(get(vi$var)[t_pts], 2))
          names(row) <- sapply(vars_info, function(vi) vi$var)
          sce_out[[as.character(sce$scenario_num)]] <- list(name = sce$name, vals = row)
        }
      }
      sens_out[[case$name]] <- sce_out
    }
    sens_out
  }

  # ---- LaTeX table generator ----------------------------------------------
  latex_esc <- function(x) gsub("(?<!\\\\)%", "\\\\%", x, perl = TRUE)

  make_sens_latex <- function(sens_out, cases, sce_nums_show, vars_info,
                               yr_lbs, caption, label, footnote) {
    case_names  <- sapply(cases, function(c) c$name)
    n_cases     <- length(case_names)
    n_val_cols  <- length(yr_lbs) * n_cases
    total_cols  <- 2 + n_val_cols
    case_hdrs   <- paste(case_names, collapse = " & ")

    # Column spec: ll then c for each value column
    col_spec <- paste0("ll", paste(rep("c", n_val_cols), collapse = ""))

    # Multi-column year headers
    yr_span <- paste(sapply(seq_along(yr_lbs), function(yi) {
      col_start <- 3 + (yi-1)*n_cases
      col_end   <- col_start + n_cases - 1
      paste0("\\multicolumn{", n_cases, "}{c}{", yr_lbs[yi], "}")
    }), collapse = " & ")

    cmidrules <- paste(sapply(seq_along(yr_lbs), function(yi) {
      col_start <- 3 + (yi-1)*n_cases
      col_end   <- col_start + n_cases - 1
      paste0("\\cmidrule(lr){", col_start, "-", col_end, "}")
    }), collapse = " ")

    short_names <- sapply(case_names, function(n) latex_esc(trimws(gsub("\\s*[\\(=].*", "", n))))
    short_hdrs  <- paste(short_names, collapse = " & ")
    sub_hdr     <- paste(rep(short_hdrs, length(yr_lbs)), collapse = " & ")

    lines <- c(
      "\\begin{table}[htbp]", "\\centering", "\\footnotesize",
      paste0("\\caption{", latex_esc(caption), "}"),
      paste0("\\label{", label, "}"),
      "\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{%",
      paste0("\\begin{tabular}{", col_spec, "}"),
      "\\toprule",
      paste0(" & & ", yr_span, " \\\\"),
      paste0(" & & ", sub_hdr, " \\\\"),
      "\\midrule"
    )

    for (vi in vars_info) {
      lines <- c(lines,
        paste0("\\multicolumn{", total_cols, "}{l}{\\textit{",
               latex_esc(vi$label), "}} \\\\"))
      for (sn in as.character(sce_nums_show)) {
        vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_along(yr_lbs), function(yr_idx) {
          sapply(case_names, function(cn) sens_out[[cn]][[sn]]$vals[[vi$var]][yr_idx])
        }))
        sce_name <- sens_out[[case_names[1]]][[sn]]$name
        lines <- c(lines,
          paste0(" & ", latex_esc(sce_name), " & ",
                 paste(formatC(vals, format="f", digits=2), collapse=" & "), " \\\\"))
      }
      lines <- c(lines, "\\midrule")
    }
    lines[length(lines)] <- "\\bottomrule"
    lines <- c(lines,
      "\\end{tabular}%",
      "}",
      paste0("\\\\[2pt]\\footnotesize ", latex_esc(footnote)),
      "\\end{table}")
    paste(lines, collapse="\n")
  }

  # ---- Standalone writer --------------------------------------------------
  write_standalone_latex <- function(latex_body, filename) {
    doc <- c(
      "\\documentclass[a4paper]{article}",
      "\\usepackage{booktabs,caption,geometry,graphicx}",
      "\\geometry{margin=1.5cm, landscape}",
      "\\begin{document}", latex_body, "\\end{document}")
    out_path <- file.path(dir_latex, filename)
    writeLines(paste(doc, collapse="\n"), out_path)
    cat("Written:", out_path, "\n")
  }

  # Shared time points and year labels
  t_pts  <- c(L+(2030-2022)*4, L+(2035-2022)*4, L+(2040-2022)*4)
  yr_lbs <- c("2030","2035","2040")

} # end run_sensitivity
```


## Sensitivity Table: delta_PS — Hyperbolic Discount Half-Life (2yr vs 10yr)
```{r sensitivity_delta_PS}
if(run_sensitivity == 1) {

  # Half-life h (years) implies delta_PS = 0.5^(1/(4*h))
  # Central: h = 5yr (20 quarters) => delta_PS = 0.5^(1/20) ≈ 0.9659
  # Case I:  h = 2yr  (8 quarters) => delta_PS = 0.5^(1/8)  ≈ 0.9170  (faster discounting)
  # Case II: h = 10yr (40 quarters) => delta_PS = 0.5^(1/40) ≈ 0.9830  (slower discounting)
  delta_PS_base <- 0.5^(1/20)   # central value: half-life = 5 years

  cases_dp <- list(
    list(name = "Case I (HL = 2yr)",
         setup_fn = function() { delta_PS <<- 0.5^(1/8); W_PS <<- delta_PS^(1:H_PS) },
         step_fn  = NULL),
    list(name = "Central (HL = 5yr)",
         setup_fn = function() { delta_PS <<- delta_PS_base; W_PS <<- delta_PS^(1:H_PS) },
         step_fn  = NULL),
    list(name = "Case II (HL = 10yr)",
         setup_fn = function() { delta_PS <<- 0.5^(1/40); W_PS <<- delta_PS^(1:H_PS) },
         step_fn  = NULL)
  )

  vars_dp <- list(
    list(var = "GDPPLOT_R",     label = "GDP, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "EMISPLOT",      label = "Total Emissions, MtCO2e"),
    list(var = "BETA_ELECPLOT", label = "NFF Electricity Share (\\%)"),
    list(var = "DEBT_GDP",      label = "Gross Debt/GDP (\\%)"),
    list(var = "INFPLOT",       label = "CPI Inflation (\\%)")
  )

  # Scenarios 1 (Baseline), 2 (FF Ban), 4 (FF Ban + Subsidy) only
  sens_dp <- run_sens_scenarios(cases_dp, vars_dp, t_pts, yr_lbs,
                                sce_nums_show = c(1, 2, 4))

  latex_dp <- make_sens_latex(
    sens_dp, cases_dp, c(1, 2, 4), vars_dp, yr_lbs,
    caption = "Sensitivity analysis: hyperbolic discount half-life ($\\delta_{PS}$). Baseline, Fossil Fuel Ban, and Fossil Fuel Ban + Subsidy scenarios.",
    label   = "tab:sens_delta_ps",
    footnote = paste0(
      "$\\delta_{PS} = 0.5^{1/h}$ where $h$ is the half-life in quarters. ",
      "Case I: $h = 8$ (2 years), $\\delta_{PS} \\approx 0.917$; ",
      "Central: $h = 20$ (5 years), $\\delta_{PS} \\approx 0.966$; ",
      "Case II: $h = 40$ (10 years), $\\delta_{PS} \\approx 0.983$. ",
      "Applies to Scenarios 1, 2 and 4 only."
    )
  )
  cat(latex_dp)
  write_standalone_latex(latex_dp, "Sensitivity_delta_PS.tex")

} # end run_sensitivity
```


## Sensitivity Table: alpha_3CRNFC ±25%
```{r sensitivity_alpha3crnfc}
if(run_sensitivity == 1) {

  alpha_3CRNFC_base <- -5.00531

  # Pre-compute central-case calibrated intercepts and lock them.
  # alpha_3CRNFC enters both CR equations via the MFI leverage ratio;
  # fixing intercepts isolates the leverage sensitivity cleanly.
  for (iterations in 1:5) Parameters_InitialValues()
  alpha_0CRNFC_base <- alpha_0CRNFC
  alpha_0CRPS_base  <- alpha_0CRPS

  cases_alpha3 <- list(
    list(name = "Case I (-25%)",
         setup_fn = function() {
           alpha_3CRNFC <<- alpha_3CRNFC_base * 0.75
           alpha_0CRNFC <<- alpha_0CRNFC_base
           alpha_0CRPS  <<- alpha_0CRPS_base
         }, step_fn = NULL),
    list(name = "Central Case",
         setup_fn = function() {
           alpha_3CRNFC <<- alpha_3CRNFC_base
           alpha_0CRNFC <<- alpha_0CRNFC_base
           alpha_0CRPS  <<- alpha_0CRPS_base
         }, step_fn = NULL),
    list(name = "Case II (+25%)",
         setup_fn = function() {
           alpha_3CRNFC <<- alpha_3CRNFC_base * 1.25
           alpha_0CRNFC <<- alpha_0CRNFC_base
           alpha_0CRPS  <<- alpha_0CRPS_base
         }, step_fn = NULL)
  )

  vars_alpha3 <- list(
    list(var = "GDPPLOT_R",  label = "GDP, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "GCF_GRPLOT", label = "Green Investment, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "EMISPLOT",   label = "Total Emissions, MtCO2e"),
    list(var = "W_SPLOT",    label = "Wage Share (\\%)"),
    list(var = "CR_NFCPLOT", label = "NFC Credit Rationing (\\%)"),
    list(var = "CR_PSPLOT",  label = "PS Credit Rationing (\\%)"),
    list(var = "DEBT_GDP",   label = "Gross Debt/GDP (\\%)")
  )

  sens_alpha3 <- run_sens_scenarios(cases_alpha3, vars_alpha3, t_pts, yr_lbs,
                                    sce_nums_show = 1:9)

  latex_alpha3 <- make_sens_latex(
    sens_alpha3, cases_alpha3, 1:9, vars_alpha3, yr_lbs,
    caption = "Sensitivity analysis: banking sector leverage coefficient ($\\alpha_{3,CRNFC}$). Effects on selected variables across all scenarios.",
    label   = "tab:sens_alpha3crnfc",
    footnote = paste0(
      "Case I = $\\alpha_{3,CRNFC} \\times 0.75$ ($-3.754$); Central = $-5.005$; Case II = $\\alpha_{3,CRNFC} \\times 1.25$ ($-6.257$). ",
      "$\\alpha_{0,CRNFC}$ and $\\alpha_{0,CRPS}$ held fixed at central-case calibrated values. ",
      "A larger magnitude coefficient increases sensitivity of credit rationing to MFI leverage."
    )
  )
  cat(latex_alpha3)
  write_standalone_latex(latex_alpha3, "Sensitivity_alpha3CRNFC.tex")

} # end run_sensitivity
```


## Sensitivity Table: alpha_1GCFNFC ±25% (Utilisation Responsiveness of Investment)
```{r sensitivity_alpha1gcfnfc}
if(run_sensitivity == 1) {

  alpha_1GCFNFC_base <- 0.029485

  # alpha_1GCFNFC governs how strongly deviations of capacity utilisation from
  # target feed into desired investment for BOTH the NFC and PS sectors:
  #   GCF_NFCD[t] = (alpha_0GCFNFC + alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_NFC_W[t-1]-u_T) + ...) * K_NFC[t-1]
  #   GCF_PSD[t]  = (alpha_0GCFPS  + alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_PS_W[t-1] -u_T) + ...) * K_PS[t-1]
  #
  # alpha_0GCFNFC and alpha_0GCFPS are calibrated as functions of alpha_1GCFNFC
  # in Parameters_InitialValues(), so they must be recalibrated inside setup_fn
  # after alpha_1GCFNFC is overridden — using the same formula as the model.

  for (iterations in 1:5) Parameters_InitialValues()

  cases_gcfnfc <- list(
    list(name = "Case I (-25%)",
         setup_fn = function() {
           alpha_1GCFNFC <<- alpha_1GCFNFC_base * 0.75
           alpha_0GCFNFC <<- (GCF_NFCD[L]/K_NFC[L-1]) - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_NFC_W[L]-u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*(GCF_NFCD[L-1]/K_NFC[L-2])
           alpha_0GCFPS  <<- GCF_PSD[L]/K_PS[L-1]   - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_PS_W[L] -u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*GCF_PSD[L-1]/K_PS[L-2]
         }, step_fn = NULL),
    list(name = "Central Case",
         setup_fn = function() {
           alpha_1GCFNFC <<- alpha_1GCFNFC_base
           alpha_0GCFNFC <<- (GCF_NFCD[L]/K_NFC[L-1]) - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_NFC_W[L]-u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*(GCF_NFCD[L-1]/K_NFC[L-2])
           alpha_0GCFPS  <<- GCF_PSD[L]/K_PS[L-1]   - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_PS_W[L] -u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*GCF_PSD[L-1]/K_PS[L-2]
         }, step_fn = NULL),
    list(name = "Case II (+25%)",
         setup_fn = function() {
           alpha_1GCFNFC <<- alpha_1GCFNFC_base * 1.25
           alpha_0GCFNFC <<- (GCF_NFCD[L]/K_NFC[L-1]) - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_NFC_W[L]-u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*(GCF_NFCD[L-1]/K_NFC[L-2])
           alpha_0GCFPS  <<- GCF_PSD[L]/K_PS[L-1]   - alpha_1GCFNFC*(u_PS_W[L] -u_T) - alpha_2GCFNFC*GCF_PSD[L-1]/K_PS[L-2]
         }, step_fn = NULL)
  )

  vars_gcfnfc <- list(
    list(var = "GDPPLOT_R",      label = "GDP, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "GCF_GRPLOT",     label = "Green Investment, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "BETA_ELECPLOT",  label = "NFF Electricity Share (\\%)"),
    list(var = "EMISPLOT",       label = "Total Emissions, MtCO2e"),
    list(var = "CR_NFCPLOT",     label = "NFC Credit Rationing (\\%)"),
    list(var = "CR_PSPLOT",      label = "PS Credit Rationing (\\%)"),
    list(var = "DEBT_GDP",       label = "Gross Debt/GDP (\\%)")
  )

  sens_gcfnfc <- run_sens_scenarios(cases_gcfnfc, vars_gcfnfc, t_pts, yr_lbs,
                                    sce_nums_show = 1:9)

  latex_gcfnfc <- make_sens_latex(
    sens_gcfnfc, cases_gcfnfc, 1:9, vars_gcfnfc, yr_lbs,
    caption = "Sensitivity analysis: utilisation responsiveness of investment ($\\alpha_{1,GCFNFC}$). Effects on selected variables across all scenarios.",
    label   = "tab:sens_alpha1gcfnfc",
    footnote = paste0(
      "$\\alpha_{1,GCFNFC}$ governs how strongly capacity utilisation gaps drive desired investment in both the NFC and PS sectors. ",
      "Case I = $\\alpha_{1,GCFNFC} \\times 0.75$ (0.022); Central = 0.029; Case II = $\\alpha_{1,GCFNFC} \\times 1.25$ (0.037). ",
      "$\\alpha_{0,GCFNFC}$ and $\\alpha_{0,GCFPS}$ are recalibrated within each case to preserve the initial investment rate."
    )
  )
  cat(latex_gcfnfc)
  write_standalone_latex(latex_gcfnfc, "Sensitivity_alpha1GCFNFC.tex")

} # end run_sensitivity
```


## Sensitivity Table: HSELL — Housing Turnover Rate (Half / Double)
```{r sensitivity_HSELL}
if(run_sensitivity == 1) {

  # HSELL is the quarterly rate at which the existing inefficient housing stock
  # turns over under regulation (S5/S6). Central = 0.012 (set by scenario).
  # step_fn overrides the scenario-set value each active simulation period.
  # For S1 (HIBAN = 0) HSELL has no effect, so S1 is identical across cases.

  cases_hsell <- list(
    list(name = "Case I (HSELL = 0.006)",
         setup_fn = function() {},
         step_fn  = function() { HSELL <<- 0.006 }),
    list(name = "Central (HSELL = 0.012)",
         setup_fn = function() {},
         step_fn  = NULL),
    list(name = "Case II (HSELL = 0.024)",
         setup_fn = function() {},
         step_fn  = function() { HSELL <<- 0.024 })
  )

  vars_hsell <- list(
    list(var = "GCF_HHHIERPLOT", label = "Green Housing Investment, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "EMISPLOT",       label = "Total Emissions, MtCO2e"),
    list(var = "BETA_ELECPLOT",  label = "NFF Electricity Share (\\%)"),
    list(var = "GDPPLOT_R",      label = "GDP, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "INFPLOT",        label = "CPI Inflation (\\%)"),
    list(var = "DEBT_GDP",       label = "Gross Debt/GDP (\\%)")
  )

  sens_hsell <- run_sens_scenarios(cases_hsell, vars_hsell, t_pts, yr_lbs,
                                   sce_nums_show = c(1, 5, 6))

  latex_hsell <- make_sens_latex(
    sens_hsell, cases_hsell, c(1, 5, 6), vars_hsell, yr_lbs,
    caption = "Sensitivity analysis: housing stock turnover rate (HSELL). Baseline, Housing Regulation, and Housing Regulation + Subsidy scenarios.",
    label   = "tab:sens_hsell",
    footnote = paste0(
      "HSELL is the quarterly rate at which inefficient housing stock turns over under regulation. ",
      "Central = 0.012 (as set by scenarios 5--6). Case I = 0.006 (half); Case II = 0.024 (double). ",
      "Applies to Scenarios 1, 5 and 6 only; HSELL has no effect in Scenario 1 (regulation flags off)."
    )
  )
  cat(latex_hsell)
  write_standalone_latex(latex_hsell, "Sensitivity_HSELL.tex")

} # end run_sensitivity
```


## Sensitivity Table: CO_NFF — NFF Investment Crowding-Out (0.25 / 0.50 / 0.75)
```{r sensitivity_CO_NFF}
if(run_sensitivity == 1) {

  # CO_NFF scales the crowding-out of private NFF investment by government
  # green spending: GCF_PSNFF[t] -= CO_NFF * SPEND_GVTPS[t].
  # setup_fn sets the value before simulation; S9's parameters() then overrides
  # it to 0 each period (by design — no step_fn used here).
  # Default in Parameters_InitialValues is 0.5.

  cases_conff <- list(
    list(name = "Case I (CO\\_NFF = 0.25)",
         setup_fn = function() { CO_NFF <<- 0.25 },
         step_fn  = NULL),
    list(name = "Central (CO\\_NFF = 0.50)",
         setup_fn = function() { CO_NFF <<- 0.50 },
         step_fn  = NULL),
    list(name = "Case II (CO\\_NFF = 0.75)",
         setup_fn = function() { CO_NFF <<- 0.75 },
         step_fn  = NULL)
  )

  vars_conff <- list(
    list(var = "GCF_NFFTOTPLOT", label = "NFF Power Investment (PS + GVT), \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "BETA_ELECPLOT",  label = "NFF Electricity Share (\\%)"),
    list(var = "GDPPLOT_R",      label = "GDP, \\pounds bn (2022p)"),
    list(var = "CR_PSPLOT",      label = "PS Credit Rationing (\\%)"),
    list(var = "DEF_PSPLOT",     label = "PS Default Rate (\\%)")
  )

  sens_conff <- run_sens_scenarios(cases_conff, vars_conff, t_pts, yr_lbs,
                                   sce_nums_show = 1:9)

  latex_conff <- make_sens_latex(
    sens_conff, cases_conff, 1:9, vars_conff, yr_lbs,
    caption = "Sensitivity analysis: NFF investment crowding-out coefficient (CO\\_NFF). Baseline, Green Public Investment, and GPI + Green Bonds scenarios.",
    label   = "tab:sens_conff",
    footnote = paste0(
      "CO\\_NFF scales the displacement of private NFF power investment by government green spending. ",
      "Case I = 0.25 (partial crowd-out); Central = 0.50; Case II = 0.75 (near-full crowd-out). ",
      "Scenario 8 hard-sets CO\\_NFF = 0 (no crowd-out by design) and is shown as reference."
    )
  )
  cat(latex_conff)
  write_standalone_latex(latex_conff, "Sensitivity_CO_NFF.tex")

} # end run_sensitivity
```


## Sensitivity Table: sh_rGVTNMFIG — Green Bond Interest Rate Premium (0 vs 1)
```{r sensitivity_sh_rGVTNMFIG}
if(run_sensitivity == 1) {

  # sh_rGVTNMFIG interpolates the green bond rate between the BoE base rate
  # (sh_r = 0) and the standard long government rate (sh_r = 1).
  # S9 sets sh_rGVTNMFIG = 0; step_fn enforces each case value.
  # GREEN_BONDS = 0 in S1 and S8, so the rate has no effect there.

  cases_shrgvt <- list(
    list(name = "Base Rate (sh\\_r = 0)",
         setup_fn = function() { sh_rGVTNMFIG <<- 0 },
         step_fn  = function() { sh_rGVTNMFIG <<- 0 }),
    list(name = "Govt Rate (sh\\_r = 1)",
         setup_fn = function() { sh_rGVTNMFIG <<- 1 },
         step_fn  = function() { sh_rGVTNMFIG <<- 1 })
  )

  vars_shrgvt <- list(
    list(var = "DEF_GDP",  label = "Fiscal Deficit/GDP (\\%)"),
    list(var = "DEBT_GDP", label = "Gross Debt/GDP (\\%)")
  )

  sens_shrgvt <- run_sens_scenarios(cases_shrgvt, vars_shrgvt, t_pts, yr_lbs,
                                    sce_nums_show = c(1, 8, 9))

  latex_shrgvt <- make_sens_latex(
    sens_shrgvt, cases_shrgvt, c(1, 8, 9), vars_shrgvt, yr_lbs,
    caption = "Sensitivity analysis: green bond interest rate premium (sh\\_rGVTNMFIG). Baseline, Green Public Investment, and GPI + Green Bonds scenarios.",
    label   = "tab:sens_shrgvt",
    footnote = paste0(
      "sh\\_rGVTNMFIG interpolates the green bond coupon rate: 0 = BoE base rate, 1 = standard long government rate. ",
      "The parameter only affects financing costs when GREEN\\_BONDS = 1 (Scenario 9). ",
      "Scenarios 1 and 8 are shown as reference (GREEN\\_BONDS = 0; rate has no effect)."
    )
  )
  cat(latex_shrgvt)
  write_standalone_latex(latex_shrgvt, "Sensitivity_sh_rGVTNMFIG.tex")

} # end run_sensitivity
```


# Validation
```{r}
if(validation==1){
  old_val_plots <- list.files(dir_validation, pattern = "\\.png$", full.names = TRUE)
  if(length(old_val_plots) > 0) file.remove(old_val_plots)

###################
#Necessary commands
###################
library(Cairo)
library(mFilter)
height_graph=800
width_graph=1.625*height_graph
res_graph=0.3* height_graph
Line1=1
Line2=3
Width1=1.5
Width2=0.8
hp_freq=400


#########
#Real data
#########
#Logarithms
log_vars <- list(
  LGDP_R = log(GDP_R[1:132]),
  LGDP_R2 = log(GDP_R[13:132]),
  LGDP_R3 = log(GDP_R[53:132]),
  LGCF_R = log(GCF_R[1:132]),
  LCONS_R = log(CONS_R[1:132]),
  LCONS_GVTR = log(CONS_GVTR[1:132]),
  LEMP = log(EMP[1:132]),
  Llambda = log(lambda[1:132]),
  LE = log(E[1:132]),
  LEMIS = log(EMIS[13:132]),
  LEXP = log(EXP_R[1:132]),
  LIMP = log(IMP_R[1:132]),
  LIBLGVT = log(IBL_GVT[53:132]/P_P[53:132]),
  LIBLNFC = log(IBL_NFC[53:132]/P_P[53:132])
)

# Load required package (if not already installed)
if (!require("mFilter")) install.packages("mFilter")
library(mFilter)

# Apply HP filter to each logged variable
hp <- lapply(log_vars, function(x) {
  hpfilter(x, freq = hp_freq, drift = TRUE)
})

#Auto-correlations
cycles_list <- lapply(hp, function(x) x$cycle)

# Compute ACF for each cycle (no plots, lag.max=20)
acf_results <- lapply(cycles_list, function(cycle) {
  acf(cycle, lag.max = 12, type = "correlation", plot = FALSE)
})

# Extract the benchmark cycles from 'hp'
benchmark_Y <- hp$LGDP_R$cycle    # Main benchmark (realY.hp$cycle)
benchmark_Y2 <- hp$LGDP_R2$cycle  # Secondary benchmark (realY.hp3$cycle)
benchmark_Y3 <- hp$LGDP_R3$cycle  # Secondary benchmark (realY.hp3$cycle)

ccf_pairs <- list(
  LGCF_R     = list(x = hp$LGCF_R$cycle,     benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  LCONS_R    = list(x = hp$LCONS_R$cycle,    benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  LCONS_GVTR = list(x = hp$LCONS_GVTR$cycle, benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  LEMP       = list(x = hp$LEMP$cycle,       benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  Llambda    = list(x = hp$Llambda$cycle,    benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  LE         = list(x = hp$LE$cycle,         benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  LEXP       = list(x = hp$LEXP$cycle,       benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  LIMP       = list(x = hp$LIMP$cycle,       benchmark = benchmark_Y),
  LEMIS      = list(x = hp$LEMIS$cycle,      benchmark = benchmark_Y2),
  LIBLGVT      = list(x = hp$LIBLGVT$cycle,   benchmark = benchmark_Y3),
  LIBLNFC      = list(x = hp$LIBLNFC$cycle,   benchmark = benchmark_Y3)
)

ccf_results <- lapply(ccf_pairs, function(pair) {
  ccf(
    x = pair$x,
    y = pair$benchmark,
    lag.max = 8,
    type = "correlation",
    plot = FALSE
  )
})

# Extract cycles (assuming 'hp' contains all filtered series)
cycles <- lapply(hp, function(x) x$cycle)

volatility <- lapply(cycles, function(cycle) sd(100 * cycle))

volatility_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(volatility),
  Volatility = unlist(volatility),
  row.names = NULL
)

##############
#Simulated data
##############
log_vars_sim <- list(
  LGDP_R = log(GDP_R[145:196]),
  LGCF_R = log(GCF_R[145:196]),
  LCONS_R = log(CONS_R[145:196]),
  LCONS_GVTR = log(CONS_GVTR[145:196]),
  LEMP = log(EMP[145:196]),
  Llambda = log(lambda[145:196]),
  LE = log(E[145:196]),
  LEMIS = log(EMIS[145:196]),
  LEXP = log(EXP_R[145:196]),
  LIMP = log(IMP_R[145:196]),
  LIBLGVT = log(IBL_GVT[145:196]/P_P[145:196]),
  LIBLNFC = log(IBL_NFC[145:196]/P_P[145:196])
)

# 1. Apply HP filter to each logged variable
hp_sim <- lapply(log_vars_sim, function(x) {
  hpfilter(x, freq = hp_freq, drift = TRUE)
})

# 2. Auto-correlations (ACF)
cycles_list_sim <- lapply(hp_sim, function(x) x$cycle)

# Compute ACF for each cycle (no plots, lag.max=20)
acf_results_sim <- lapply(cycles_list_sim, function(cycle) {
  acf(cycle, lag.max = 12, type = "correlation", plot = FALSE)
})

# 3. Cross-correlations (CCF)
benchmark_Y_sim <- hp_sim$LGDP_R$cycle    # Main benchmark
benchmark_Y2_sim <- hp_sim$LGDP_R$cycle   # Using same benchmark for all in simulated data

ccf_pairs_sim <- list(
  LGCF_R     = list(x = hp_sim$LGCF_R$cycle,     benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LCONS_R    = list(x = hp_sim$LCONS_R$cycle,    benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LCONS_GVTR = list(x = hp_sim$LCONS_GVTR$cycle, benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LEMP       = list(x = hp_sim$LEMP$cycle,       benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  Llambda    = list(x = hp_sim$Llambda$cycle,    benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LE         = list(x = hp_sim$LE$cycle,         benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LEMIS      = list(x = hp_sim$LEMIS$cycle,      benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LEXP       = list(x = hp_sim$LEXP$cycle,     benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LIMP       = list(x = hp_sim$LIMP$cycle,        benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LIBLGVT    = list(x = hp_sim$LIBLGVT$cycle,        benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim),
  LIBLNFC    = list(x = hp_sim$LIBLNFC$cycle,        benchmark = benchmark_Y_sim)
)

ccf_results_sim <- lapply(ccf_pairs_sim, function(pair) {
  ccf(
    x = pair$x,
    y = pair$benchmark,
    lag.max = 8,
    type = "correlation",
    plot = FALSE
  )
})

# 4. Volatility calculation
cycles_sim <- lapply(hp_sim, function(x) x$cycle)

volatility_sim <- lapply(cycles_sim, function(cycle) sd(100 * cycle))

volatility_df_sim <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(volatility_sim),
  Volatility = unlist(volatility_sim),
  row.names = NULL
)

# 5. Create comparison plots (similar to your benchmark style)
height_graph <- 800
width_graph <- 1.625 * height_graph
res_graph <- 0.3 * height_graph
Line1 <- 1  # For simulated data
Line2 <- 3  # For actual data (if you want to compare)
Width1 <- 1.5
Width2 <- 0.8

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# ================
# Helper function: ACF comparison
# ================
plot_acf_comparison <- function(var_name, acf_data_real, acf_data_sim, filename) {
  # Prepare data
  df_real <- data.frame(
    Lag = acf_data_real$lag,
    Value = acf_data_real$acf,
    Type = "Actual"
  )
  df_sim <- data.frame(
    Lag = acf_data_sim$lag,
    Value = acf_data_sim$acf,
    Type = "Simulated"
  )
  df <- bind_rows(df_real, df_sim)
  
  # Force y-axis within [-1,1] for autocorrelations
  ymin <- -1
  ymax <- 1
  
  # Plot
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Lag, y = Value, color = Type)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 1.8, stroke = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Simulated" = "blue", "Actual" = "red")) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, by = 2), limits = c(0, 12)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.2), limits = c(ymin, ymax)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      text = element_text(family = "serif"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black"),
      legend.position = c(0.85, 0.9),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
      legend.key = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", size = 0.8),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(size = 0.2, linetype = 'solid', colour = "grey80"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_blank()   # <--- removes titles
    ) +
    labs(x = "Lag", y = "Autocorrelation")

  ggsave(file.path(dir_validation, filename), p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}


# ================
# Helper function: CCF comparison
# ================
plot_ccf_comparison <- function(var_name, ccf_data_real, ccf_data_sim, filename) {
  # Prepare data
  df_real <- data.frame(
    Lag = ccf_data_real$lag,
    Value = ccf_data_real$acf,
    Type = "Actual"
  )
  df_sim <- data.frame(
    Lag = ccf_data_sim$lag,
    Value = ccf_data_sim$acf,
    Type = "Simulated"
  )
  df <- bind_rows(df_real, df_sim)
  
  # Cross-correlations bounded [-1,1]
  ymin <- -1
  ymax <- 1
  
  # Plot
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Lag, y = Value, color = Type)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 1.8, stroke = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Simulated" = "blue", "Actual" = "red")) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-8, 8, by = 2), limits = c(-8, 8)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-1, 1, by = 0.2), limits = c(ymin, ymax)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      text = element_text(family = "serif"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black"),
      legend.position = c(0.85, 0.9),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
      legend.key = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", size = 0.8),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(size = 0.2, linetype = 'solid', colour = "grey80"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_blank()   # <--- removes titles
    ) +
    labs(x = "Lag", y = "Cross-correlation")

  ggsave(file.path(dir_validation, filename), p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}


# ================
# Loops
# ================
# ACF plots
for (var_name in names(acf_results_sim)) {
  plot_acf_comparison(
    var_name,
    acf_results[[var_name]],
    acf_results_sim[[var_name]],
    filename = paste0("ACF_comparison_", var_name, ".png")
  )
}

# CCF plots
for (var_name in names(ccf_results_sim)) {
  plot_ccf_comparison(
    var_name,
    ccf_results[[var_name]],
    ccf_results_sim[[var_name]],
    filename = paste0("CCF_comparison_", var_name, "_vs_GDP.png")
  )
}

# Print volatility results
cat("=== SIMULATED DATA VOLATILITY ===\n")
print(volatility_df_sim)

# Save all results to RDS files for later comparison
saveRDS(list(
  hp_sim = hp_sim,
  acf_results_sim = acf_results_sim,
  ccf_results_sim = ccf_results_sim,
  volatility_df_sim = volatility_df_sim
), file.path(dir_validation, "simulated_analysis_results.rds"))

cat("\nAnalysis complete. Results saved to:\n")
cat("- ACF plots for each variable\n")
cat("- CCF plots against GDP for each variable\n")
cat("- Volatility data frame\n")
cat("- RDS file with all results\n")
}
```

# Exporting Model Results

<!-- Plots are generated per block inside the run_blocks chunk above. -->

## Export Model to LaTeX

### Count the Equations

```{r}
if(latex==1){
code_lines<-capture.output(Equations) 
extract_variables<-function(code_lines) {
  valid_lines<-grep("<<-", code_lines, value = TRUE)
  matches<-regmatches(valid_lines, regexpr("^[^\\[]+", valid_lines))
  return(matches)
}
variables<-extract_variables(code_lines)
Equations_numbering<-cbind(Variable=variables, Equation = seq_along(variables))
Equations_numbering<- as.data.frame(Equations_numbering, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
Equations_numbering$Variable <- trimws(Equations_numbering$Variable)
Equations_numbering$Equation <- trimws(Equations_numbering$Equation)
}
```

### LaTeX Table with Initial Values for Endogenous Variables

```{r}
if(latex==1){
########################################################
#Create a table with the symbols and the initial values
########################################################
column_names<-colnames(Variables_sorted_Baseline)
first_row_values<-signif(Variables_sorted_Baseline[L, ],4)
transposed_first_row_values<-t(first_row_values)
Table_variables<-cbind(column_names,transposed_first_row_values)
colnames(Table_variables)<-c("Symbol","Initial value")
rownames(Table_variables) <- NULL
Table_variables <- as.data.frame(Table_variables)
############################
#Add the description column
############################
code_lines <- capture.output(Parameters_InitialValues) 
extract_comment <- function(var, code_lines) {
  line_with_comment <- grep(paste0("\\b", var, "\\[1:L\\] <<-"), code_lines, value = TRUE)
  if (length(line_with_comment) > 0) {
    comment <- sub(".*#\\s*(.*?)\\s*##.*", "\\1", line_with_comment)
    return(trimws(comment))  
  } else {
    return(NA)  
  }
}
Table_variables$Description<- sapply(Table_variables$Symbol, extract_comment, code_lines= code_lines)
Table_variables <- Table_variables[!is.na(Table_variables$Description) & Table_variables$Description != "", ]

Table_variables<-Table_variables[, c("Symbol", "Description", "Initial value")]
############################
#Add the category column
############################
code_lines <- capture.output(Parameters_InitialValues) 
free_start <- grep("#Free", code_lines)
model_constrained_start <- grep("#Model-constrained", code_lines)
free_vars <- character(0)
model_constrained_vars <- character(0)
# Extract variables from 'Free' section
if (length(free_start) > 0) {
  next_start <- min(c(model_constrained_start, length(code_lines) + 1))
  free_lines <- code_lines[(free_start + 1):(next_start - 1)]
  free_vars <- grep("<<-", free_lines, value = TRUE)
  free_vars <- sub("\\s*<<-.*", "", free_vars)  
  free_vars <- gsub("\\[.*", "", free_vars)  
  free_vars <- trimws(free_vars)  
}

# Extract variables from 'Model-constrained' section
if (length(model_constrained_start) > 0) {
  model_constrained_lines <- code_lines[(model_constrained_start + 1):length(code_lines)] 
  model_constrained_vars <- grep("<<-", model_constrained_lines, value = TRUE)
  model_constrained_vars <- sub("\\s*<<-.*", "", model_constrained_vars)  
  model_constrained_vars <- gsub("\\[.*", "", model_constrained_vars)  
  model_constrained_vars <- trimws(model_constrained_vars) 
}

# Function to categorise the variables
categorize_variables <- function(var) {
  if (var %in% free_vars) {
    return("Free")
  } else if (var %in% model_constrained_vars) {
    return("Model-constrained")
  } else {
    return(NA) 
  }
}

Table_variables$Category <- sapply(Table_variables$Symbol, categorize_variables)
Table_variables <- Table_variables[, c("Symbol", "Description", "Category", "Initial value")]
colnames(Table_variables)[colnames(Table_variables) == "Category"] <- "Variable category"

##############################
#Add the source/remarks column 
##############################
code_lines <- capture.output(Parameters_InitialValues) 
extract_comment <- function(var, code_lines) {
  line_with_comment <- grep(paste0("\\b", var, "\\[1:L\\] <<-"), code_lines, value = TRUE)
  if (length(line_with_comment) > 0) {
    comment <- sub(".*##\\s*([^#]*?)\\s*$", "\\1", line_with_comment)
    return(trimws(comment))  
  } else {
    return(NA)  
  }
}
Table_variables$Sources<- sapply(Table_variables$Symbol, extract_comment, code_lines= code_lines)
Table_variables<-Table_variables[, c("Symbol", "Description", "Variable category", "Initial value","Sources")]
colnames(Table_variables)[colnames(Table_variables) == "Sources"] <- "Source/remarks"
##########################################################
#Add the number of equations in the source/remarks column
##########################################################
replace <- function(Table_variables, Equations_numbering) {
  lookup <- setNames(as.character(Equations_numbering[, 2]), Equations_numbering[, 1])
  variable_pattern <- paste0("\\b(", paste(names(lookup), collapse = "|"), ")\\b")
  Table_variables[, 5] <- sapply(Table_variables[, 5], function(x) {
    if (is.na(x)) {
      return("")  
    }
    x <- trimws(x)
    for (var in names(lookup)) {
      if (grepl(var, x)) {
        x <- gsub(paste0("\\b", var, "\\b"), lookup[var], x)
      }
    }
    return(x)
  })
  return(Table_variables)
}
Table_variables<- replace(Table_variables, Equations_numbering)
##########################
#Create the table in latex
##########################
Table_variables$Symbol<- paste0("$", Table_variables$Symbol, "$")
Latex_table_variables<- xtable(Table_variables)
Latex_code <- capture.output(print(Latex_table_variables,  include.rownames = FALSE, sanitize.colnames.function = function(x) paste0("\\textbf{", x, "}"), type = "latex"))
Latex_code<-gsub("\\\\verb\\|([^|]+)\\|", "\\1", Latex_code)
Latex_code<-gsub("_(\\w+)", "_{\\1}", Latex_code, perl=TRUE)
Latex_code<-gsub("\\^(\\w+)", "^{\\1}", Latex_code, perl=TRUE)
Latex_code<-gsub("\\\\\\$", "\\$", Latex_code)
Latex_code<-gsub("\\\\_", "_", Latex_code)
Latex_code<-gsub("(\\w+_\\{\\w+\\})", "\\$\\1\\$", Latex_code)
Latex_code<-gsub("(\\w+\\^\\{[^}]+\\})", "\\$\\1\\$", Latex_code)
Latex_code<-gsub("\\$\\$", "\\$", Latex_code)

for (greek in greek_letters) {
  
  Latex_code <- gsub(
    paste0("\\$(.*?)", "(?<![a-zA-Z0-9])", greek, "(?![a-zA-Z0-9])", "(.*?)\\$"), 
    paste0("\\$", "\\\\", greek, "\\$", "\\1\\2"), 
    Latex_code, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE
  )
  Latex_code <- gsub(paste0("(", greek, ")(_)([a-zA-Z0-9]+)"), 
                     paste0("\\\\", greek, "_{\\3}"), Latex_code)
  Latex_code <- gsub(paste0("(", greek, ")(_\\{)([a-zA-Z0-9]+)(\\})"), 
                     paste0("\\\\", greek, "_{\\3}"), Latex_code)
  Latex_code <- gsub("\\\\b\\\\eta", "\\\\beta", Latex_code)
  Latex_code <- gsub("th\\\\eta", "\\theta", Latex_code)
  Latex_code <- gsub(paste0("(", greek, ")(?=\\s)"), 
                     paste0("\\\\", greek), Latex_code, perl = TRUE)
  Latex_code <- gsub(paste0("(", greek, ")(?=[A-Z])"), 
                     paste0("\\\\", greek, " "), Latex_code, perl = TRUE)
  Latex_code <- gsub(paste0("(", greek, ")(?=\\$)"), 
                     paste0("\\\\", greek), Latex_code, perl = TRUE)
  Latex_code <- gsub(paste0("\\\\", greek), 
                     paste0("\\", greek), Latex_code)
  Latex_code <- gsub(paste0("(\\$\\\\", greek, ")\\$(_\\{[^}]+\\})"),
                     "\\1\\2\\$",Latex_code, perl = TRUE)
  
}

# Remove table wrapper and convert to longtable
Latex_code <- Latex_code[!grepl("\\\\begin\\{table\\}|\\\\end\\{table\\}|\\[ht\\]|^\\s*$", Latex_code)]
Latex_code <- gsub("\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}\\{lllll\\}", "\\\\begin{longtable}{p{2cm}p{4.5cm}p{2cm}p{2cm}p{4.5cm}}", Latex_code)
Latex_code <- gsub("\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\begin{longtable}", Latex_code)
Latex_code <- gsub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{longtable}", Latex_code)

# Find the header row (contains \textbf) and add longtable repeat commands
header_idx <- grep("\\\\textbf", Latex_code)[1]
if (!is.na(header_idx)) {
  header_row <- Latex_code[header_idx]
  # Add strut for vertical spacing in header row
  header_row_spaced <- gsub("^(\\\\textbf)", "\\\\rule{0pt}{14pt}\\1", header_row)
  # Insert longtable header commands after the header row
  # Format: \hline, header row, \hline for continuation pages
  longtable_header_cmds <- c(
    "\\endfirsthead",
    "\\hline",
    header_row_spaced,
    "\\hline",
    "\\endhead",
    "\\hline",
    "\\endfoot",
    "\\hline",
    "\\endlastfoot"
  )
  # Insert after the header row and its following \hline
  if (grepl("\\\\hline", Latex_code[header_idx + 1])) {
    insert_pos <- header_idx + 1
  } else {
    insert_pos <- header_idx
  }
  Latex_code <- c(Latex_code[1:insert_pos], longtable_header_cmds, Latex_code[(insert_pos+1):length(Latex_code)])
}

Latex_preamble <- "
\\documentclass{article}
\\usepackage[a4paper, left=1.5cm, right=1.5cm, top=2.5cm, bottom=2.5cm]{geometry}
\\usepackage{longtable}
\\usepackage{fancyhdr}
\\usepackage{lastpage}
\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{1.3}

\\pagestyle{fancy}
\\fancyhf{}
\\fancyhead[L]{\\textbf{UK SFC Model -- Endogenous Variables}}
\\fancyhead[R]{\\thepage\\ of \\pageref{LastPage}}
\\renewcommand{\\headrulewidth}{0.4pt}
\\renewcommand{\\footrulewidth}{0pt}

\\begin{document}
"
Latex_footer <- "
\\end{document}
"
Full_latex_code <- paste(Latex_preamble, paste(Latex_code, collapse = "\n"), Latex_footer, sep = "\n")
writeLines(Full_latex_code, file.path(dir_latex, "Latex_Table_variables.tex"))
}
```

### LaTeX Table with Parameter Values

```{r}
if(latex==1){
########################################################
#Create a table with the symbols and the parameter values
########################################################
column_names<-colnames(Parameters_sorted_Baseline)
first_row_values<-signif(Parameters_sorted_Baseline[1, ],4)
transposed_first_row_values<-t(first_row_values)
Table_parameters<-cbind(column_names,transposed_first_row_values)
colnames(Table_parameters)<-c("Symbol","Value")
rownames(Table_parameters)<- NULL
Table_parameters<-as.data.frame(Table_parameters)
Table_parameters$Symbol<-trimws(Table_parameters$Symbol)
############################
#Add the description column
############################
code_lines<-capture.output(Parameters_InitialValues) 
extract_comment<-function(par, code_lines) {
  line_with_comment <- grep(paste0("\\b", par,"\\s*<<-"), code_lines, value = TRUE)
  if (length(line_with_comment) > 0) {
    comment <- sub(".*#\\s*(.*?)\\s*##.*", "\\1", line_with_comment)
    return(trimws(comment))  
  } else {
    return(NA)  
  }
}
Table_parameters$Description<- sapply(Table_parameters$Symbol, extract_comment, code_lines= code_lines)
Table_parameters <- Table_parameters[!is.na(Table_parameters$Description) & Table_parameters$Description != "", ]

Table_parameters<-Table_parameters[, c("Symbol", "Description", "Value")]
########################
#Add the category column
########################
code_lines <- capture.output(Parameters_InitialValues) 
free_start <- grep("#Free", code_lines)
data_determined_start <- grep("#Data-determined", code_lines)
model_constrained_start <- grep("#Model-constrained", code_lines)
free_pars <- character(0)
model_constrained_pars <- character(0)

# Extract variables from 'Free' section
if (length(free_start) > 0) {
  next_start <- min(c(model_constrained_start, length(code_lines) + 1))
  free_lines <- code_lines[(free_start + 1):(next_start - 1)]
  free_pars <- grep("<<-", free_lines, value = TRUE)
  free_pars <- sub("\\s*<<-.*", "", free_pars)  
  free_pars <- trimws(free_pars)  
}


# Extract variables from 'Model-constrained' section
if (length(model_constrained_start) > 0) {
  model_constrained_lines <- code_lines[(model_constrained_start + 1):length(code_lines)] 
  model_constrained_pars <- grep("<<-", model_constrained_lines, value = TRUE)
  model_constrained_pars <- sub("\\s*<<-.*", "", model_constrained_pars)  
  model_constrained_pars <- trimws(model_constrained_pars) 
}

# Function to categorize the parameters
categorize_parameters <- function(par) {
  if (par %in% free_pars) {
    return("Free")
  } else if (par %in% model_constrained_pars) {
    return("Model-constrained")
  } else {
    return(NA) 
  }
}

Table_parameters$Category<-sapply(Table_parameters$Symbol, categorize_parameters)
Table_parameters<-Table_parameters[, c("Symbol", "Description", "Category", "Value")]
colnames(Table_parameters)[colnames(Table_parameters) == "Category"] <- "Parameter category"
##############################
#Add the source/remarks column 
##############################
code_lines<-capture.output(Parameters_InitialValues) 
extract_comment<-function(par, code_lines) {
  line_with_comment <- grep(paste0("\\b", par,"\\s*<<-"), code_lines, value = TRUE)
  if (length(line_with_comment) > 0) {
    comment <- sub(".*##\\s*([^#]*?)\\s*$", "\\1", line_with_comment)
    return(trimws(comment))  
  } else {
    return(NA)  
  }
}

Table_parameters$Sources<- sapply(Table_parameters$Symbol, extract_comment, code_lines= code_lines)
Table_parameters<-Table_parameters[, c("Symbol", "Description", "Parameter category", "Value","Sources")]
colnames(Table_parameters)[colnames(Table_parameters) == "Sources"] <- "Source/remarks"

##########################################################
#Add the number of equations in the source/remarks column
##########################################################
replace <- function(Table_parameters, Equations_numbering) {
  lookup <- setNames(as.character(Equations_numbering[, 2]), Equations_numbering[, 1])
  variable_pattern <- paste0("\\b(", paste(names(lookup), collapse = "|"), ")\\b")
  Table_parameters[, 5] <- sapply(Table_parameters[, 5], function(x) {
    if (is.na(x)) {
      return("")  
    }
    x <- trimws(x)
    for (var in names(lookup)) {
      if (grepl(var, x)) {
        x <- gsub(paste0("\\b", var, "\\b"), lookup[var], x)
      }
    }
    return(x)
  })
  return(Table_parameters)
}
Table_parameters<- replace(Table_parameters, Equations_numbering)

####################################
#Create the table in Latex
####################################
Table_parameters$Symbol<- paste0("$", Table_parameters$Symbol, "$")
Latex_table_parameters<- xtable(Table_parameters)
```
